source.text:
Alice Nguyen: Getting a spike of 403s on /scim/v2/Users deprovision for a handful of Okta tenants. Started ~09:12 UTC, saw ~50 failures in 5m. Anyone else?
Ben Carter: Saw it at 09:13. Correlates with last night's KMS alias swap. Quick grep shows JWE_DECRYPT_FAILED around that window.
Priya Singh: I'm pulling assertion logs and SCIM audit. Noting that some SAML assertions are missing the 'roles' attribute after yesterday's RBAC mapping change.
Samir Patel: App side shows JWE decrypt errors and then RBAC denies:
```
2026-03-21T09:12:45Z ERROR auth.jwt: JWE_DECRYPT_FAILED alias=redwood-kms-v2 key_id=ks_abc123 err=decryption failed
2026-03-21T09:12:45Z WARN scim.deprovision: deprov failed user=acct-42 customer=acme-corp status=403 request_id=req_789
```
Priya Singh: KMS audit shows decrypt calls returned OK for our service principal, but token verifier yields 403 because claim 'roles' is absent and the RBAC gate rejects.
Ben Carter: Can someone curl the SCIM endpoint with a freshly minted token from a repro session to confirm body?
Priya Singh: Repro token returns 403 and body: {"error":"forbidden","reason":"missing role:scim_deprov"}. The assertion contained groups but not the mapped 'roles' claim.
Alice Nguyen: We should enable full tracing for idp.assertion.* and scim.deprovision.* and alert infra. Also draft a customer note.
oncall-rotation: I'm oncall, escalating to infra for immediate alias rebind to previous key. Creating an incident.
idp-bot: Incident created: INC-2026-0042. Responders: @oncall-rotation, @Priya Singh, @Samir Patel.
Ben Carter: From vuln-triage view: no crypto exploit observed; likely timing/regression: RBAC attribute mapping change + KMS alias rotation overlapped.
Priya Singh: Short-term mitigation: add a feature-flagged fallback in token verifier to accept legacy claim shapes (e.g., look for 'groups' -> map to 'roles') for SCIM deprovision while we rollback/repair. Limit to 1 hour.
Samir Patel: I'll open a PR for the verifier fallback and a staging canary. If infra can rebind alias we can test quickly.
Alice Nguyen: Add structured audit events: scim.deprovision.fail and idp.assertion.missing_role with customer_id, request_id, token_kid, and affected_user. We'll need these for postmortem.
oncall-rotation: Assigning: Samir -> rollback PR + canary, Ben -> logs & correlation, Priya -> mapping fix + test plan, Alice -> customer comms and postmortem lead.
Ben Carter: Pushed a log-collector change to staging to count missing-role occurrences by tenant.
Priya Singh: After infra rollback and RBAC mapping revert, confirm deprovision succeeds for acme-corp + two other tenants.
Samir Patel: Rollback PR merged to staging, deploying now. Patch is behind a feature flag; canary in 10m.
Alice Nguyen: Root cause hypothesis: RBAC attribute mapping change removed a required claim at the same time a KMS alias rotation introduced a transient key-binding mismatch. Together caused a wave of 403s.
Priya Singh: Action items: immediate alias rollback, revert mapping, add verifier fallback, add monitoring for missing role claims, update runbook to prevent alias rotations during provisioning windows, and add a postmortem. I'll also start a vuln-triage note to verify no security regression introduced by the fallback.
oncall-rotation: I'll send a brief advisory to affected customers and update the incident timeline. Thanks all. :thumbsup:
Alice Nguyen: Good catch everyone. Keep updates in this thread and paste the final timeline into the postmortem doc.
Priya Singh: Note for postmortem: include exact timestamps for alias rotation, RBAC rollout, and first 403s; include affected tenant list and counts.
Samir Patel: Will attach canary logs and k8s rollout IDs once done.
Ben Carter: Collecting logs and will upload to INC-2026-0042.
Alice Nguyen: Closing loop: once rollback stable, schedule a follow-up meeting to discuss safer rollout gating.
Priya Singh: Marking follow-up: RBAC mapping gate + KMS alias post-checks.
oncall-rotation: Incident state -> investigating -> mitigated (pending postmortem).

source.text:
Priya Singh: Noticed a surge of 403s on /scim/v2/Users deprovision around 09:12 UTC for several Okta customers.
Samir Patel: Saw same. App logs show JWE_DECRYPT_FAILED spikes.
Ben Carter: Correlates with last-night KMS alias rotation + RBAC attribute mapping deploy.
Priya Singh: Assertion payloads missing mapped 'roles' claim; verifier rejects -> 403.
oncall-rotation: I'm oncall. Escalating infra to consider alias rollback.
Samir Patel: Opening a quick fallback PR to accept legacy group->role mapping behind a flag. Canary to staging first.
Ben Carter: From vuln-triage: no crypto exploit; looks like timing/config race.
Priya Singh: Enable audit events: idp.assertion.missing_role and scim.deprov.fail with tenant_id and request_id. Need data for postmortem.
oncall-rotation: Incident INC-2026-0042 created. Assigning tasks: Samir (fallback PR), Ben (logs/correlation), Priya (mapping revert + tests).
Priya Singh: Short-term mitigation: rollback alias and revert mapping, then validate deprovision for affected tenants.
Samir Patel: PR merged to staging, canary passing. Patch is time-limited.
Ben Carter: Collecting counts by tenant; will attach logs to incident.
Priya Singh: Postmortem action items: block alias rotations during provisioning windows, add black/white list for claim shapes, and add monitoring for missing-role rates.
oncall-rotation: Updating customers and will post timeline. :thumbsup:

source.text:
Alice Nguyen: Heads up — getting a spike of 403s on /scim/v2/Users deprovision for a few Okta customers. Started ~09:12 UTC. Anyone else seeing this?
Ben Carter: Yep, saw it ~09:13. Correlates with last night's KMS alias swap.
Priya Singh: I'm pulling audit logs now. Quick note: a few assertions are missing the 'roles' attribute after the RBAC mapping change we rolled yesterday.
Samir Patel: App logs show some JWE decrypt failures around the same time:
```
2026-03-21T09:12:45Z ERROR auth.jwt: JWE_DECRYPT_FAILED alias=redwood-kms-v2 key_id=ks_abc123 err=decryption failed
2026-03-21T09:12:45Z WARN scim.deprovision: deprov failed user=acct-42 customer=acme-corp status=403
```
Priya Singh: Hmm — KMS logs show decrypt calls succeeded but our token verifier returns 403 because claim 'roles' is absent and our RBAC check denies deprovision.
Ben Carter: Can someone curl the SCIM endpoint with a token from a repro session? Quick sanity:
```bash
curl -v -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" https://api.redwood.example/scim/v2/Users/123
```
We get 403 with body: {"error":"forbidden","reason":"missing role:scim_deprov"}
Alice Nguyen: We should enable full audit-level tracing for idp.assertion.* and scim.deprovision.* and notify infra. Temporary rollback plan?
oncall-rotation: :eyes: I'm oncall. Escalating to infra to rebind alias to previous key id. Creating incident now.
idp-bot: Incident created: INC-2026-0042. Responders: @oncall-rotation, @Priya Singh, @Samir Patel.
Ben Carter: From vuln-triage side — nothing looks like a crypto vulnerability. Likely config/regression caused by RBAC attribute mapping change + KMS alias timing.
Priya Singh: Proposed mitigation: allow a fallback path that accepts legacy key IDs/claim shapes for SCIM deprov while we roll back and remap attributes. Feature-flagged, 1 hour max.
Samir Patel: I'll open a PR for the fallback + staging canary. If infra can rebind alias, we can test immediately.
Alice Nguyen: Add audit events for scim.deprovision.fail and idp.assertion.missing_role, include customer_id and request_id. We need these in the postmortem.
oncall-rotation: Assigning: Samir -> rollback PR + canary, Ben -> logs & correlation, Priya -> audit mapping fix, Alice -> customer communications + postmortem lead. Notifying affected customers with template.
Ben Carter: Pushed a quick log-collector change to staging to surface missing claim counts.
Priya Singh: After rollback to previous alias and audit mapping revert, confirm deprovision for acme-corp and two other affected tenants.
Samir Patel: Rollback PR ready, deploying to staging now. Will log results in the incident.
Alice Nguyen: Root cause hypothesis: RBAC attribute mapping change removed a required claim at the same time a KMS alias rotation created a transient key-binding mismatch. Combined led to 403 wave. Action items: immediate rollback, postmortem, update runbook to block alias rotations during critical provisioning windows, add monitoring for missing role claims.
Priya Singh: Will add the runbook items and start a vuln-triage note to confirm no security regression introduced.
oncall-rotation: Thanks everyone. I'll follow up with incident update and a short customer advisory. :thumbsup:

source.text:
Priya Desai: Heads up — we're scheduling retirement of the Legacy Token (v1) used by older mobile SDKs and a handful of partners. Need a short design for a compatibility bridge (shim), telemetry plan, and an escape hatch. Cutting without this will cause auth failures.
Alex Kim: How big is the surface? tenants + traffic numbers?
Priya Desai: Rough numbers: ~12 partner tenants, ~3 mobile apps on SDK <=3.2.1. Peak auth calls ~30k/day; steady ~8–10k/day.
Mateo Alvarez: Proposal: run an auth-bridge sidecar at the edge that accepts the old token, performs a limited exchange to a v2 bearer (server-side) and injects Authorization: Bearer <v2>. Keeps core services unchanged. Alternative: map headers in the proxy but sidecar gives better telemetry and rate-limiting.
Jen Harris: Security notes: the bridge must validate signature+claims, add TTL, and emit audit events (legacy_token=true). We cannot accept unsigned tokens indefinitely. Also require key material in Vault/KMS and strict rotation.
Alex Kim: Rollout idea: feature-flag gated per-tenant: `compat_legacy_token`. Start Canary 1% -> 10% -> 50% -> 100% over 2w. Announce deprecation 90 days before hard cut (document + email + SDK update).
Priya Desai: We'll also ship an SDK helper to call the exchange when it sees an old token: `rw_auth.exchangeLegacyToken()` — try-local-first, fallback to bridge. Push migration to mobile app owners in first week.
Mateo Alvarez: Example nginx+lua snippet to forward legacy token to bridge endpoint: ```
# in edge proxy
set $legacy_token $http_x_legacy_token;
if ($legacy_token) {
  proxy_pass http://auth-bridge.internal/exchange;
  proxy_set_header X-Legacy-Token $legacy_token;
}
```
Jen Harris: Good. Also add escape hatch header `X-RW-Compat-Force: legacy-v1` that bypasses strict reject for known support windows, but only enabled via tenant flag and audited. Add metric `legacy_token.exchange.failures` and alert if >1% of auth volume for 10m.
Alex Kim: Tests: add integration test that uses a malformed legacy token to confirm 401, and a stale-but-signed token to confirm 422 with clear error. Validate metrics emitted: `legacy_token.requests`, `legacy_token.success`, `legacy_token.failures`.
Priya Desai: Docs: deprecation page + migration checklist for partners + SDK migration snippet. Timetable: announce tomorrow, enable canary next Tue, widen over 2w, full deprecate earliest 90 days post-announce.
Mateo Alvarez: Key handling: bridge uses dedicated keypair stored in Vault; exchange endpoint will validate incoming v1 signature against rotated verification keys we publish to partner docs. Rotate bridge keys every 30 days and log rotations.
Jen Harris: Ops: require auditing hooks into Console (tag events with `legacy_token=true`) so SRE/EngSec can query. Add a runbook for rollback: toggle `compat_legacy_token` off per-tenant + global kill-switch.
ops-bot: deploy-bot started canary deployment for `auth-bridge` in edge-us-west-2 at 4132110180.

title: #eng-security: 4132110000
preview:
Priya Desai: Heads up — we're scheduling retirement of the Legacy Token (v1) used by older mobile SDKs and a handful of partners. Need a short design for a compatibility bridge (shim), telemetry plan, and an escape hatch. Cutting without this will cause auth failures.
Alex Kim: How big is the surface? tenants + traffic numbers?
Priya Desai: Rough numbers: ~12 partner tenants, ~3 mobile apps on SDK <=3.2.1. Peak auth calls ~30k/day; steady ~8–10k/day.
Mateo Alvarez: Proposal: run an auth-bridge sidecar at the edge that accepts the old token, performs a limited exchange to a v2 bearer (server-side) and injects Authorization: Bearer <v2>. Keeps core services unchanged. Alternative: map headers in the proxy but sidecar gives better telemetry and rate-limiting.
Jen Harris: Security notes: the bridge must validate signature+claims, add TTL, and emit audit events (legacy_token=true). We cannot accept unsigned tokens indefinitely. Also require key material in Vault/KMS and strict rotation.
Alex Kim: Rollout idea: feature-flag gated per-tenant: `compat_legacy_token`. Start Canary 1% -> 10% -> 50% -> 100% over 2w. Announce deprecation 90 days before hard cut (document + email + SDK update).
Priya Desai: We'll also ship an SDK helper to call the exchange when it sees an old token: `rw_auth.exchangeLegacyToken()` — try-local-first, fallback to bridge. Push migration to mobile app owners in first week.
Mateo Alvarez: Example nginx+lua snippet to forward legacy token to bridge endpoint: ```
# in edge proxy
set $legacy_token $http_x_legacy_token;
if ($legacy_token) {
  proxy_pass http://auth-bridge.internal/exchange;
  proxy_set_header X-Legacy-Token $legacy_token;
}
```
Jen Harris: Good. Also add escape hatch header `X-RW-Compat-Force: legacy-v1` that bypasses strict reject for known support windows, but only enabled via tenant flag and audited. Add metric `legacy_token.exchange.failures` and alert if >1% of auth volume for 10m.
Alex Kim: Tests: add integration test that uses a malformed legacy token to confirm 401, and a stale-but-signed token to confirm 422 with clear error. Validate metrics emitted: `legacy_token.requests`, `legacy_token.success`, `legacy_token.failures`.
Priya Desai: Docs: deprecation page + migration checklist for partners + SDK migration snippet. Timetable: announce tomorrow, enable canary next Tue, widen over 2w, full deprecate earliest 90 days post-announce.
Mateo Alvarez: Key handling: bridge uses dedicated keypair stored in Vault; exchange endpoint will validate incoming v1 signature against rotated verification keys we publish to partner docs. Rotate bridge keys every 30 days and log rotations.
Jen Harris: Ops: require auditing hooks into Console (tag events with `legacy_token=true`) so SRE/EngSec can query. Add a runbook for rollback: toggle `compat_legacy_token` off per-tenant + global kill-switch.
ops-bot: deploy-bot started canary deployment for `auth-bridge` in edge-us-west-2 at 4132110180.
deploy-bot: canary traffic routing enabled -> 1% -> monitoring enabled.
Alex Kim: Who's owning the partner comms? Priya? Also need a partner acceptance test and a sandbox for them to validate.
Priya Desai: I'll own comms and the sandbox. Action items: 1) Mateo implement bridge + exchange API, 2) Jen add metrics/alerts+runbook, 3) Alex add feature-flag gating + tests, 4) Priya partner comms + SDK helper + docs.
Jen Harris: Also coordinate with legal for any contract language for 90-day deprecation windows.
Mateo Alvarez: I'll raise a PR for the bridge proto and a minimal exchange endpoint tomorrow.
Priya Desai: Thanks all. We'll reconvene after canary metrics look stable. Reaction: :thumbsup:

source.thread:
Rosa Kim: spotted a short base64 blob in partner-telemetry logs from svc-ingest-5. Looks like a wrapped ciphertext fragment. Line: `2026-03-22T02:14:18Z id=pt-77 payload=Q3J5cHRvX2Jsb2JfZ3JheQ==`

Maya Singh: which bucket/service emitted it?

Rosa Kim: bucket: s3://telemetry-ingest/2026/03/22/, service: telemetry-ingest-5, container: uploader. Only one file contained it.

Ethan Park: quick checklist: 1) search for that base64 across all buckets 2) pull Vault transit/audit entries around that time 3) check that svc-ingest-5 hasn't used any secret write perms recently.

ops-bot: grep across logs: 1 hit (same file). No other matches.

vault-bot: vault-audit: no transit/decrypt calls for partner key in 02:00..03:00Z. KMS-proxy: no successful rewraps, one 400 from svc-ingest-5 at 02:13:50Z.

Priya Patel: treat as potential exposure. Immediate steps: rotate transit key for partner, revoke any short-lived tokens from svc-ingest-5 that can write secret material, and scrub the captured object.

Maya Singh: I'll open RBAC patch to deny `write` to secret mounts for svc-ingest-5 pending investigation.

Ethan Park: revoking token for svc-ingest-5 now. Command: `gcloud iam service-accounts keys disable --key-file=...` (done).

Rosa Kim: beginning secure-archive: copying original to s3://secure-archive/incidents/3801239999/ then replacing with masked stub.

Maya Singh: propose adding a runtime log scrubber to drop/mask base64 payloads > 48 chars in webhook and telemetry paths. I'll PR kyverno-like rule and a small middleware change.

Priya Patel: rewrap plan: rotate `transit/partner-telemetry-wrap` to new version, then run rewrap job for any stored ciphertexts. We'll queue via our rewrap-worker with concurrency 2.

Ethan Park: I'll do a staging dry-run first. Also collect CloudTrail + Vault audit into incident folder.

ops-bot: secure-archive copy complete. Masked stub now in place.

vault-bot: staging dry-run rewrap (50 items) -> 0 failures. Ready to schedule prod rewrap.

Maya Singh: PR opened for mask middleware: https://git.internal/ingest/mr/101 (includes tests).

Priya Patel: scheduling prod rotate at 04:15Z and rewrap at 04:30Z. Notifying oncall.

Rosa Kim: partner has been notified via secure channel and asked to rotate any client-side keys and check their upload path.

Ethan Park: prod rotate + rewrap completed at 04:48Z. 123 objects rewrapped, 0 errors. Post-run spot-checks successful.

Priya Patel: collecting timeline and logs in s3://redwood-incidents/3801239999/. Drafting short postmortem; will include remediation: masking, RBAC guard, and SIEM alert for base64 payloads.

Maya Singh: can we add an automated sanitizer to ingress to prevent future raw captures? I can prototype next sprint.

Rosa Kim: thanks all. Closing once postmortem posted and partner confirms rotations. :white_check_mark:

source.messages:
Security Ops: FYI team — rolling out a mandatory deprecation of reusable service API keys. Any key that is reused across >1 service or has TTL >24h will be disabled after a 10-day remediation window. See actions below. :warning:

Ethan Park: Quick: does this hit keys in shared vault paths or only plaintext in configs?

Security Ops: Both. Shared vault paths are OK if the secret is automatically rotated to <=24h or issued per-service. Plaintext or duplicated keys in configs are in-scope and must be replaced with per-service short-lived creds or OIDC.

QA Infra: Our test harness uses a shared token across environments. Can we get an exemption?

Security Ops: Temporary exceptions allowed but need a ticket with justification and mitigation (IP allowlist, restricted network, expiration date). File in #eng-sre with tag "shortlived-exception". Exceptions reviewed within 48h.

SRE oncall: Migration tips: prefer issuing STS tokens per job, or use the proxy service pattern. Example vault role for ephemeral AWS creds:
```
vault write auth/aws/role/ci-role \
  auth_type=iam \
  policy=ci-policy \
  ttl=1h
```
Also remove any hard-coded keys in repos — search: `git grep -n "AKIA"` and rotate.\nsecrets-bot: automated-scan: Found 3 services referencing shared-key-qa in vault path secret/ci/shared (owners: qa-team). Please remediate.

Ethan Park: Will split tokens per-environment and update configs today.

Security Ops: Action checklist: 1) Stop sharing keys across services, 2) Move to per-service short-lived creds (<=24h), 3) Rotate any static keys now, 4) Open exception ticket if unable to migrate. Dry-run notifications next Wednesday; enforcement in 10 days.

QA Infra: Acknowledged, filing exception for one legacy runner now.

SRE oncall: Happy to pair on vault role setup — ping me.

Security Ops: Thanks — we'll publish a short how-to and the search queries to find reused keys in the org.

source.thread:
Mira Patel: morning — async standup:

Mira Patel: Yesterday: finished IAM role inventory (exported CSV to s3), rotated keys for svc-account-llm, filed PR to tighten least-priv policies. Blocker: can't update KMS key policy because org-admin approval pending (ticket INC-482).

Alex Chen: saw the inventory, left inline comments on the PR. For the KMS approval, add me as provisional approver and I’ll escalate.

Sam Ortiz: ran vuln scan yesterday, confirmed CVE-2025-1234 is present in non-prod only. Patch PR up with tests: https://git.company/repo/pull/412. Blocker: need access to staging KMS replica to rewrap secrets.

devops-bot: Deploy report: rotation-job v2.3 started at 1800457890, completed for 12/14 keys. Failed on kms-us-west-2/prod-key (403).

Jordan Blake: I’ll take the 403 — looks like policy drift from last week's TF change, patching now.

Lee Morgan: Compliance note: finishing SOC docs — need evidence of key rotations for last 90 days. Mira, can you attach the S3 export + rotation-job logs to INC-482 once available?

Mira Patel: attaching logs now: s3://redwood-inference-logs/iam/rotation-180045/rotation.log (redacted). Also added a KMS ticket update with the account to escalate.

Alex Chen: quick ask — can reviewers please look at alex/iam-minimum-roles PR (link above). Needs one more approval before merge. If you've got 10 min please skim :pray:

Sam Ortiz: reviewing now. Small nit on iam: kms:ReEncrypt action scope — will comment.

Jordan Blake: patched TF, re-applied key policy. Devops bot, can you rerun the rotation?

Jordan Blake: kubectl exec -n infra rotation-job -- /bin/sh -c 'scripts/rotate-keys --retry'  (running on my laptop)

devops-bot: rotation retry queued (job id rtry-1800460200). I'll post completion status.

Lee Morgan: for SOC evidence I'll need CSV with key_id,timestamp,initiator once rotation completes. Mira, can you generate that?

Mira Patel: will generate and attach by EOD. Thanks everyone.

Nina Lopez: Heads up — Enterprise Security (CloudBank) sent a 30-question SQ focused on incident evidence: they want an incident replay showing how we revoke access and how KMS envelopes are tracked. can we assemble a packet?
Ben Carter: which scope do they want? tenant-level events or org-wide?
Nina Lopez: they specifically asked for tenant-scoped timeline for the affected tenant plus an overview of org controls.
Arjun S.: I can extract tenant-scoped audit events. prefer CSV or JSONL for playback?
Leah M.: JSONL is better for playback demo, CSV for summary. include role bind/unbind, session create/revoke, and kms:Encrypt/decrypt ops.
ops-bot: started job job-ephem-179999 (eta 90s) extracting: sessions, role_bindings, kms_ops, token_mints.
Ben Carter: red-team note: they exploited a long-lived support SA that wasn't on the rotation schedule. timeline and corrective actions are required.
Nina Lopez: add an RCA section: root cause, timeline, short-term mitigation, long-term policy changes.
Arjun S.: snippet to fetch recent KMS envelope metadata: ```sh
redwoodctl kms list-envelopes --tenant T-EXAMPLE --since 2025-11-01 --fields envelope_id,key_version,operation
```
Leah M.: do not include ciphertext in any export. include envelope metadata + how to verify signature.
Ben Carter: for the demo we should obfuscate PII and tenant IDs. CloudBank wants evidence they can verify though. propose: include signed manifest and a verification script.
Nina Lopez: agreed. include a small verification script that checks manifest signature and timestamps.
Arjun S.: ops-bot produced artifacts: s3://redwood-sec/exports/1799998888/audit_playback.jsonl (signed), and s3://redwood-sec/exports/1799998888/rbac_history.csv.
Leah M.: also prepare a snippet of the emergency revocation commands for the call: ```sh
# emergency revoke
redwoodctl iam revoke-session --session S-20251110-XYZ
redwoodctl iam remove-binding --tenant T-EXAMPLE --principal svc:stale-support --role tenant-admin
```
Ben Carter: CloudBank asked about streaming logs to their SIEM. we support push to S3 or direct syslog/HTTP sink with signed bundles. include sample exporter config.
Nina Lopez: sample exporter: ```yaml
export:
  type: http
  endpoint: https://siem.cloudbank.example/receiver
  auth: sigv4
  signed_archives: true
```
Leah M.: retention note: default audit retention is 365 days; enterprise agreements can extend retention up to 7 years. put that in the packet.
Arjun S.: on token policy changes: we'll add exp claim enforcement starting now and make SA rotation 24h by default. include policy link and rollout timeline.
Ben Carter: add a short note about IP redaction: we obfuscate last octet by default for shared demos, full IPs available under NDA.
Nina Lopez: I'll draft the cover email and the incident appendix (RCA + mitigation timeline). Leah/Arjun/Ben please review before I send. target to send EOD PT.
ops-bot: reminder: security-review call scheduled: 2025-12-03T17:00Z.
Leah M.: I can join the call and run the playback. Ben: can you prepare the verification script?
Ben Carter: on it. will push script to the shared folder. :white_check_mark:
Nina Lopez: thanks team. once reviewed i'll send and add a calendar invite for the walk-through.

source.messages:
Samir Patel: Heads up — we're finalizing logistics for the simulated assessment next month. Proposed window: Apr 12–14 UTC. Goal: scope infra boundaries, KMS handling, RBAC tests, audit log coverage. @Ava Chen can you confirm KMS key rotation windows? :calendar:
Ava Chen: I can, but need to check with infra. Current rotation is daily for ephemeral keys, monthly for root. For customer-managed keys we need to confirm HSM availability. I'll follow up today.
Jared Nguyen: For audit logs we should ensure user-level RBAC events are captured in syslog and pushed to SIEM. Query we typically use:
```
index=redwood_audit sourcetype=rbac action=* | stats count by actor, action, resource
```
Ava Chen: Good idea. Also need to add a flag to not export KMS plaintext with logs — should be redacted at source. @sec-bot can you attach last 30 days of log schema?
sec-bot: Attached: audit_schema_v3.json (link) — parity notes: event_type, actor_id, resource_id, action, before, after, metadata
Samir Patel: Great. For RBAC changes during testing we want frozen policy baseline + ephemeral test policies. Proposed approach:
- create test role `sim_assess_role` with time-bound grants
- use PR workflow against `infra/iam-policies` repo
- require 2 reviewers from eng-security before merge
Ava Chen: Agree. We'll need an automated rollback hook if any test triggers risky operations.
Jared Nguyen: On remediation tracking — suggest we open a single parent ticket in JIRA `SEC-SIM-2026` and create subtasks per finding. Can we get an SLA for remediations? 7 days for high, 30 for medium?
Samir Patel: Yeah, 7/30 sounds right. Also need to schedule a retest window: May 1–3. We'll inform assessors to focus on previously remediated items first.
Ava Chen: I'll coordinate with customer-managed KMS teams; pinging infra-oncall to confirm HSM access: @infra-oncall. :phone:
infra-oncall: HSM access scheduled for Apr 11 06:00–18:00 UTC. We can provide temporary key material. Please submit access request by Apr 4.
Jared Nguyen: Noted. I'll draft the onboarding checklist and attach to the tracker: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/SEC-SIM-2026 :spiral_note_pad:
Samir Patel: Thanks — can everyone add estimated owners to the checklist by EOD tomorrow? :eyes:
Ava Chen: Will do.
sec-bot: Reminder: please ensure all VPC flow logs are enabled before test start — automated check in 48h.

Samir Patel: Thanks — can everyone add estimated owners to the checklist by EOD tomorrow? :eyes:
Ava Chen: Will do.

source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick ping — opened PR #4122 that changes how the on-prem agent fetches secrets and updates auth token rotation. Need security eyes and VPC/network implications checked before we merge.
Samir Patel: Thanks Ava — linking the PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/repo/pull/4122. Will take a look in ~2 hrs. What's the risk window if we delay merge?
Ava Chen: If we wait a day it's fine for staging. Prod rollout is scheduled next Tue; prefer to have approvals before Friday so QA can run the on-prem smoke tests.
Priya Nair: High-level: code touches secret fetcher + adds a new `auth_proxy` config flag that can be set to `local` or `vpc`. Need to ensure no plaintext logging and KMS usage is enforced for `vpc` mode.
Ava Chen: Yep — added a migration: `agent.config` gains `auth_proxy: vpc|local` and the code path for vpc uses `kms_key_id`. Small diff snippet in PR:

```diff
+ if (config.auth_proxy === 'vpc') {
+   const token = await kms.decrypt(config.kms_key_id, wrappedToken);
+ }
```

Ava Chen: Also added tests but want a security read on threat model for a partially-misconfigured VPC (route table/NAT traps).
Leo Gómez: Quick Q: does the change alter how secrets are cached in-memory? We need to avoid long-lived plaintext in RAM on multi-tenant hosts.
Ava Chen: Cache TTL unchanged (5m) but I added a `cache.encrypted` flag that uses envelope encryption when set. Default is false to avoid perf hit; we can toggle for Dedicated/Private deployments.
Priya Nair: Please add a note in PR checklist to require `cache.encrypted=true` for any on-prem install that handles customer PII. I'll add a review comment.
Samir Patel: Reviewing now. Leaving inline comments about: (1) missing error handling when KMS decrypt fails, (2) logging level may leak key IDs. Also request a small e2e on a VPC with restricted egress.
infra-bot: CI passed — unit tests 212/212, lint OK.
Ava Chen: Thanks — addressed KMS error handling and scrubbed logs. Pushed fix 1785004a. Marking for re-review @Samir @Priya. If you can approve, I'll queue canary for staging this afternoon.
Priya Nair: I'll do a security pass after lunch. If nothing critical, will approve and add the `security-approved` label.
Samir Patel: Approved from infra side, leaving a minor nit on comment formatting. +1 for merge once Priya signs off.
Ava Chen: Great — tagging release engineering for canary: @release-team. Planning canary in us-west-2 staging at 15:00 PT.
Leo Gómez: I'm on canary watch. If we see auth errors, we can rollback via `deploy rollback agent-onprem 2026-03-17-rc2`.
Ava Chen: Perfect. Will hold merge until security label. Thanks team :thumbsup:

source.text:
Alice Chen: heads-up, vendor published GPU stack advisories (driver + cuDNN). initial CVEs called out: CVE-2026-0421 (driver privilege escalation) and CVE-2026-0422 (cuDNN malformed tensor OOB). NCCL had a separate infoleak fix in 2.17.0 (CVE-2026-0550).
Marco Diaz: paste the link? need to map affected builds and our images.
Alice Chen: https://vendor.example/nvidia/advisory/2026-0421-0422 - drivers 535.86+ and cuDNN 9.2.x affected per vendor.
Priya Nair: TL;DR: high local exploitability. For hosted multitenant nodes this is HIGH (processes run as root on GPU nodes). Dedicated/private less urgent but still P1 for compliance.
Ben Carter: prioritization suggestion: Hosted: 48h emergency patch (canary -> stage -> prod). Dedicated: 30-day advisory + scheduling. Private: publish patch bundles + compatibility notes.
sec-oncall: Also ensure audit logging captures driver/package installs and package sigs. Add KMS signature verification into the deployment flow.
Alice Chen: Agreed. Action item: add post-install smoke to capture signature/hash into audit S3. Example: `rpm -qa --qf "%{NAME} %{VERSION} %{SIGPGP:pgpsig}\n"` and push to s3-audit immutable bucket.
Marco Diaz: compatibility risk: some quantized kernels compiled against older cuDNN ABI. We saw regressions on llm-inference-2.1 benchmarks last update. Need matrix: model-family x cuda/cuDNN x quant-profile.
Priya Nair: I can kick off matrix jobs. Quick QA smoke command for infra:
```
./smoke-test.sh --model gpt-2-onnx --cuda 12.1 --cudnn 9.2 --seed 42 --batch 1
```
Add variants: `--quant=4bit` and `--kvcache`.
infra-bot: Canary cluster deploy scheduled: canary-ggp-1 -> 2026-03-21T02:00Z. Rolling window 2h. Notifications enabled.
Ben Carter: owners/timeline (proposed):
- Priya: triage + full smoke matrix (6h)
- Infra: canary patch & infra smoke (12h)
- Marco: rollback validation & compat notes (24h)
- Alice/Ben: customer comms + Dedicated advisory (36h)
Alice Chen: RBAC/audit question: restrict who can trigger kernel/driver upgrades? recommend temporary `driver-upgrade` role scoped to infra-oncall + security.
sec-oncall: +1. For private tenant nodes require dual-approval and log approvals. Keep approvals in audit trail.
Priya Nair: Early matrix results: cuDNN 9.2.1 reduces the OOB behavior but introduced 3-6% p95 latency for small-seq quantized models. Perf cap: <10% regression acceptable, but evaluate cost delta.
Marco Diaz: tradeoff plan: if latency regression >10% on any high-QPS route we fallback those routes to a pinned runtime_variant with older cuDNN. We have policy routing by `runtime_variant` tag. Can we route 5% traffic to canary for 6h?
Alice Chen: yes, add canary tag `gpu_patch_canary=true` and set route to 5% for initial validation.
Ben Carter: KMS signing follow-up: vendor-signed packages exist, but we want to re-sign our internal distro. Who owns keys? Ops or Security?
sec-oncall: Security owns keys. We'll provision a signing key in HSM/KMS and grant infra temporary SignGrant for the rollout jobs. Document procedure under security/kms-driver-signing.
Priya Nair: test checklist to run across matrix:
- startup smoke (drivers load)
- forward/backward correctness with kv-cache
- NCCL multi-process allreduce sanity
- perf microbench (p50/p95)
- golden-token E2E check (token diffs)
Marco Diaz: add embedding similarity threshold and a small golden-token regression check. If quality drifts we block.
Alice Chen: draft customer advisory: short statement + mitigations (isolate GPU nodes, disable multi-tenant scheduling, enforce RBAC). I'll draft and loop in legal/security for disclosure timing.
Ben Carter: rollback playbook sketch: revert driver rpm, restore module deps, clear nvpeer caches, restart runtime. Marco to validate in staging.
Marco Diaz: validating rollback now in staging. will post results. If blocking regressions found we will hold canary and escalate.
infra-bot: Canary deploy completed. 5% traffic routed. Monitoring hooks enabled: latency, error rate, OOMs, security-audit events.
Priya Nair: canary initial: no errors except one route with quantized models showing 6% p95 increase. investigating model-specific kernel path.
sec-oncall: label audit logs with `gpu-patch-2026-0421` for easier forensics. Ensure logs are immutable for 90 days.
Alice Chen: summary/actions:
- Priya: finish matrix + perf QA (6h)
- Marco: complete rollback validation + compat report (12h)
- Ben: finalize customer advisory + Dedicated customer scheduling (36h)
- Security: KMS signing policy + audit retention config (24h)
I'll update thread when comms ready. Thanks all :thumbsup:

source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick question — if we deprovision a user via SCIM (Okta), do we strip email addresses from service logs immediately? We're getting a privacy request and need to know whether logs still contain user emails. cc @Ravi Patel @Maya Ghosh
Ravi Patel: short answer: deprovisioning removes account but logs are retained per retention policy. we don't automatically redact fields in historical logs unless a deletion job runs. :frowning:
Lin Ortega: do we have a list of which pipelines ingest raw usernames/emails? I think api-gateway and embeddings-service still log `user_email` in request metadata right now.
SecurityBot: incoming event: `SCIM: user_deactivated` for user alice@example.com at 2026-02-28T14:12:09Z — no automatic log purge scheduled.
Maya Ghosh: for this specific privacy request can we run a targeted redaction? if yes, what's the process — ticket + legal signoff?
Ava Chen: want to avoid a full retention policy change. a one-off redaction is fine if it's documented and auditable. Ravi — can you confirm ops playbook?
Ravi Patel: ops playbook: open PRIVACY-DELETE ticket, include user id + timestamp range, legal approves -> infra run `redact-logs --user-email alice@example.com --services api-gateway,embeddings-service` (this uses redact job in gcs -> bigquery). note: redact job replaces email with `<redacted>` but preserves trace_id.
Lin Ortega: will that break any linked traces in observability dashboards? we rely on email in some ad-hoc dashboards.
Maya Ghosh: good point. preference is to preserve trace_id and user_id but remove PII in message fields. legal okayed that approach in past cases.
Ava Chen: can someone post the redact job snippet? want to sanity-check before opening ticket.
Ravi Patel: snippet:
```
redact-logs \n  --filter "user_email:alice@example.com" \n  --fields user_email,message \n  --replace "<redacted>"
```
Ravi Patel: the job takes ~15m for two services; we'll snapshot and store an audit log in vault/log-redactions/<ticket-id>. :white_check_mark:
Lin Ortega: is there any automation tied to SCIM deprovision that should trigger the ticket? e.g., new deactivation webhook -> create PRIVACY-DELETE draft?
Maya Ghosh: not yet. we can add a step in the SCIM webhook handler to emit a `privacy_review_required` event when `external_system=okta` and `user_role` in `sensitive_roles`.
Ava Chen: sounds good. two asks: (1) file PRIVACY-DELETE ticket for alice@example.com with the redact job attached, (2) open a small PR to add a `privacy_review_required` webhook event on deprovision. will assign to Ravi + Lin. thanks
Ravi Patel: filing ticket now -> PRIVACY-DELETE-4427. attaching job plan. ping @Maya Ghosh for legal signoff.
Maya Ghosh: approved, + please keep audit trail. :eyes:
SecurityBot: deploy check: PR #3214 created for scim-webhook: add privacy event. CI passed.
Ava Chen: perfect — thanks all. I'll update the requester and mark as high priority.

SecurityBot: deploy check: PR #3214 created for scim-webhook: add privacy event. CI passed.
Ava Chen: perfect — thanks all. I'll update the requester and mark as high priority.
text:
Ava Chen: Quick question — if we deprovision a user via SCIM (Okta), do we strip email addresses from service logs immediately? We're getting a privacy request and need to know whether logs still contain user emails. cc @Ravi Patel @Maya Ghosh
Ravi Patel: short answer: deprovisioning removes account but logs are retained per retention policy. we don't automatically redact fields in historical logs unless a deletion job runs. :frowning:
Lin Ortega: do we have a list of which pipelines ingest raw usernames/emails? I think api-gateway and embeddings-service still log `user_email` in request metadata right now.
SecurityBot: incoming event: `SCIM: user_deactivated` for user alice@example.com at 2026-02-28T14:12:09Z — no automatic log purge scheduled.
Maya Ghosh: for this specific privacy request can we run a targeted redaction? if yes, what's the process — ticket + legal signoff?
Ava Chen: want to avoid a full retention policy change. a one-off redaction is fine if it's documented and auditable. Ravi — can you confirm ops playbook?
Ravi Patel: ops playbook: open PRIVACY-DELETE ticket, include user id + timestamp range, legal approves -> infra run `redact-logs --user-email alice@example.com --services api-gateway,embeddings-service` (this uses redact job in gcs -> bigquery). note: redact job replaces email with `<redacted>` but preserves trace_id.
Lin Ortega: will that break any linked traces in observability dashboards? we rely on email in some ad-hoc dashboards.
Maya Ghosh: good point. preference is to preserve trace_id and user_id but remove PII in message fields. legal okayed that approach in past cases.
Ava Chen: can someone post the redact job snippet? want to sanity-check before opening ticket.
Ravi Patel: snippet:
```
redact-logs 
  --filter "user_email:alice@example.com" 
  --fields user_email,message 
  --replace "<redacted>"
```
Ravi Patel: the job takes ~15m for two services; we'll snapshot and store an audit log in vault/log-redactions/<ticket-id>. :white_check_mark:
Lin Ortega: is there any automation tied to SCIM deprovision that should trigger the ticket? e.g., new deactivation webhook -> create PRIVACY-DELETE draft?
Maya Ghosh: not yet. we can add a step in the SCIM webhook handler to emit a `privacy_review_required` event when `external_system=okta` and `user_role` in `sensitive_roles`.
Ava Chen: sounds good. two asks: (1) file PRIVACY-DELETE ticket for alice@example.com with the redact job attached, (2) open a small PR to add a `privacy_review_required` webhook event on deprovision. will assign to Ravi + Lin. thanks
Ravi Patel: filing ticket now -> PRIVACY-DELETE-4427. attaching job plan. ping @Maya Ghosh for legal signoff.
Maya Ghosh: approved, + please keep audit trail. :eyes:
SecurityBot: deploy check: PR #3214 created for scim-webhook: add privacy event. CI passed.
Ava Chen: perfect — thanks all. I'll update the requester and mark as high priority.

source.body:
Markus Klein: got a customer security questionnaire asking: “How often do you perform penetration testing?” and “Describe vulnerability scanning frequency + tooling.” What’s the approved phrasing now? Don’t want to over-commit on cadence.

Marcus Lin: yep, pls don’t answer with a specific month/quarter unless you’re citing the approved snippet.

Kevin Osei: +1. We’ve had a few questionnaires lately trying to pin us to “quarterly external pen test” language.

Olga Petrov: For customer-facing answers we should stick to the approved control statement + route detailed evidence via the SOC 2 evidence process.

Marcus Lin: Here’s the current “safe” block I’ve been using (from the claims matrix / do-not-say doc):

Marcus Lin: 
```
Penetration testing: Redwood conducts periodic independent third‑party penetration tests of relevant systems and services, including after material changes where appropriate. Identified findings are tracked to remediation in accordance with Redwood’s vulnerability management process.

Vulnerability scanning: Redwood uses automated vulnerability scanning and monitoring across code, dependencies, container images, and cloud infrastructure. Scanning occurs on an ongoing basis and results are triaged and remediated based on severity.

Evidence: Executive summaries and/or relevant attestation evidence may be made available under NDA via Redwood’s compliance evidence request process.
```

Markus Klein: that’s helpful. do we have anything that says “at least annually” or is that a no-go?

Daniel Carter: We *can* say “at least annually” only if you’re using the exact approved FAQ/RFP language for the current version. Otherwise keep it “periodic/recurring.” (Some customers interpret “annually” as a contractual requirement.)

Olga Petrov: +1. Also questionnaires often split “application pen test” vs “network pen test” vs “cloud config review.” If they ask for those separately and the canned block doesn’t map cleanly, use the exceptions policy / escalate rather than improvising.

Naomi Feldman: also note: for Private deployments, customer-owned infra responsibilities come up. If the questionnaire is about their on-prem/VPC environment, we should be explicit on shared responsibility.

Fatima Noor: yep, in Private: we can speak to Redwood software + our secure SDLC and release process, but customer handles network perimeter + their own vuln scans on the environment unless contract says otherwise.

Markus Klein: this is for Dedicated (Redwood-managed). so the block above should be fine.

Kevin Osei: Dedicated/Hosted: we can say we do automated scanning across infra + images + dependencies; avoid naming specific vendors/tools in writing unless the question explicitly requests it and we have approval to disclose.

Marcus Lin: if they *require* tool names, ask them if “equivalent controls description” is acceptable. If not, kick to GRC for a tailored response.

Olga Petrov: links for reference (internal):
- Security claims matrix (pen test / vuln mgmt tabs): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/security-claims-matrix (restricted)
- “What we do/don’t say” guidance (pen test language): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SE/what-we-do-and-dont-say
- SOC 2 / evidence request playbook (NDA + artifact flow): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/GRC/soc2-customer-evidence-request-playbook
- Questionnaire exceptions policy (when they demand cadence/tooling/report): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/GRC/customer-security-questionnaire-exceptions-policy

Markus Klein: nice. can we tighten the one-liner for the questionnaire field (they only give 500 chars)?

Daniel Carter: suggested short form (fits most portals):
“Redwood performs periodic independent third‑party penetration testing of relevant systems and maintains an ongoing vulnerability management program with automated scanning across code, dependencies, container images, and cloud infrastructure. Findings are triaged and remediated based on severity. Pen test summaries/attestation evidence available under NDA upon request.”

Marcus Lin: :thumbsup: use that. If they ask for “latest pen test report,” respond with “available under NDA via evidence request process” (don’t attach from your laptop / email).

Markus Klein: perfect, thanks all. :pray:

Olga Petrov: (small add) if the portal has a yes/no for “Do you perform external pen tests at least annually?” and you’re unsure, don’t guess—ping GRC w/ the exact question + deadline.

source.text:
Marcus Lin: Dropping proposed marketplace security claims here for final accuracy check before Docs publishes + PMM puts into AWS/GCP listing. Please nitpick wording.

Claims (draft):
1) “All customer data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES‑256).”
2) “Customer content (prompts/outputs) is not used to train models.”
3) “Hosted API request/response payloads are retained for 30 days by default for abuse monitoring and debugging; customers can request shorter retention.”
4) “Audit logs are available for admin actions and API key events, and can be exported to customer SIEM.”
5) “Keys are managed via cloud KMS; Dedicated/Private customers can use customer-managed keys.”
6) “Data residency: processing occurs in the region selected by the customer (where available).”

Need to confirm what’s true across Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private, and what needs qualifiers.

Naomi Feldman: :eyes: going through one by one. Overall ok but some are over-broad. We need product-mode qualifiers or AWS/GCP reviewers will bounce it.

Naomi Feldman: 1) “AES‑256 at rest” is usually fine but we should say “encrypted at rest using industry-standard encryption” unless we can prove every storage layer is AES‑256 (object store, DB, backups, logs). Also “TLS 1.2+” ok, but note some internal service-to-service is mTLS—don’t overspec.

Olga Petrov: +1. From compliance standpoint, over-specific crypto claims create evidence burden. Better: “encrypted at rest and in transit” and keep TLS 1.2+ if we have a standard.

Marcus Lin: Got it. We can change to “TLS 1.2+” + “encryption at rest” without naming AES. Or “AES‑256 where supported by underlying cloud services”?

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Don’t do “where supported” — reads weak. Prefer: “Data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest using industry-standard encryption. Encryption is provided by major cloud provider managed services for Hosted.”

Elliot Price: For docs, I can implement mode-specific callouts: Hosted / Dedicated / Private. Listing copy is tighter though. We can keep listing generic and link to full security page.

Alex Martinez: +1, marketplace pages can be brief and point to the “Marketplace Security Overview” doc. But the claims on listing still need to be defensible.

Marcus Lin: 2) “not used to train models” — true for customer content? I know we don’t train foundation models, but do we use any prompts/outputs for eval/quality monitoring if customer opts in?

Naomi Feldman: We have an opt-in program for sharing data for quality improvements (explicit). Default is no. Wording should be: “We do not use customer content to train Redwood or third-party foundation models without explicit customer permission.”

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Yes. Avoid absolute “never” statements. Add “without explicit permission” / “unless customer opts in.”

Daniel Carter: From evidence POV, we can point to policy + DPA language. But the marketing claim should match the policy exactly.

Marcus Lin: 3) Retention: “30 days by default” — is that accurate for Hosted logs? I recall payload retention is shorter in prod unless debug is enabled.

Priya Natarajan: Looping in (platform). For Hosted: request metadata (timings, token counts, error codes) retained longer for billing/analytics. Full payload retention is *not* 30 days universally. We store payloads only for a subset of traffic for abuse detection + customer support debugging, and it’s configurable per tenant. Default is currently 7 days for payload sampling, 30 days for derived logs/metrics. (reaction: :warning: x3)

Naomi Feldman: This is exactly why we need to split “payload” vs “metadata.” Marketplace reviewers also ask specifically about “customer content.” Payload == customer content.

Marcus Lin: Ok so claim 3 as written is wrong. Proposed rewrite:
- “By default, Redwood retains limited request metadata for billing and operational monitoring. Customer content (prompt/response payloads) is not retained except as necessary for abuse prevention and support, and retention can be configured by plan.”

Alex Martinez: That’s closer. We should also provide a concrete number somewhere in the security doc, but maybe not in the listing. Buyers ask “how many days” though.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Put the exact defaults in the security packet (versioned) and keep listing language high-level. If defaults change, we don’t want to update marketplace listings every time.

Olga Petrov: +1. Also, ensure we mention “deletion within X days of retention expiration” if we have that in policy.

Marcus Lin: 4) Audit logs: We definitely log admin actions + API key events in console. Do we have guaranteed export to SIEM today or is it “available via API” and customer builds integration?

Priya Natarajan: Audit log export: currently available via API + S3 sink for Dedicated (feature flag). Hosted has API access; SIEM integrations are “customer managed” (Splunk, etc.) via webhook/collector on their side. No turnkey “one-click Splunk app.”

Elliot Price: So the claim should be: “Audit logs can be accessed via the Redwood Console and API; export options available (plan-dependent).”

Naomi Feldman: Yes, and list events: “org membership changes, role changes, API key create/revoke, policy changes, model deployment/rollout actions.” Don’t claim “all events.”

Marcus Lin: 5) KMS + CMK: “Keys are managed via cloud KMS; Dedicated/Private can use customer-managed keys.” True for Dedicated? I think we support CMK for certain storage only.

Priya Natarajan: Dedicated: CMK supported for object storage + log storage when deployed in customer VPC / dedicated environment, not for every internal service. Private (customer-controlled) can integrate with customer KMS/HSM depending on deployment.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Wording suggestion:
- “Hosted: encryption keys are managed by Redwood using cloud-provider key management services.”
- “Dedicated/Private: customer-managed keys are supported for select storage systems; details available in security packet.”

Olga Petrov: “select storage systems” is safe. Also avoid “HSM” unless we can deliver it across clouds consistently.

Marcus Lin: 6) Data residency: “processing occurs in region selected” — but fallback routing could cross-region if customer enables it, right?

Naomi Feldman: Yes. Also Dedicated/Private are pinned, Hosted has region choice but some control-plane metadata might be global.

Priya Natarajan: Correct. In Hosted, control plane is multi-region but some auth/billing metadata replicated. We do not move payload cross-region unless customer has multi-region failover enabled. Need that nuance.

Alex Martinez: Suggest listing language:
- “Hosted supports region selection. Data is processed in-region by default. Optional multi-region failover can be enabled.”
Then security doc has the full “what is in-region vs global metadata.”

Marcus Lin: Summarizing edits we’ll apply:
- Remove AES‑256 mention; keep “encrypted at rest” + “TLS 1.2+”
- Training: add “without explicit permission/opt-in”
- Retention: remove “30 days payload” claim; split metadata vs content; move concrete defaults to security packet
- Audit logs: “available in console + API; export options plan-dependent; event types listed; not ‘all events’”
- KMS/CMK: qualify to Hosted vs Dedicated/Private + “select systems”
- Residency: “processed in-region by default; note control-plane metadata + optional failover”

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Approved direction. Please also add “shared responsibility” sentence somewhere (marketplace reviewers expect it). Something like “Redwood secures the platform; customers are responsible for securing their accounts, API keys, and client-side usage.”

Olga Petrov: And ensure the security overview references SOC 2 Type II coverage without implying certification scope beyond what we have. Use “SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA/on request.”

Elliot Price: I’ll update the docs PR copy accordingly and tag @Marcus Lin @Naomi Feldman for final pass. Also will add a “Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private” table so we stop mixing claims.

Alex Martinez: Once wording is updated, please paste the final “approved marketplace-safe copy block” back here so PMM can lift it directly.

Marcus Lin: Will do. I’ll post the revised block later today after I integrate Priya’s notes on retention + residency.

Priya Natarajan: If helpful I can drop exact current defaults for (a) payload sampling retention (b) metrics retention (c) audit log retention per plan. But agree—keep it in packet, not listing.

Naomi Feldman: :thumbsup: Also please sanity check that “TLS 1.2+” matches our edge config everywhere (incl. older SDKs). If any endpoints are still negotiating 1.2 only, still fine, but don’t claim 1.3.

Marcus Lin: Ack. I’ll verify with edge config and keep it “TLS 1.2+” only.

Daniel Carter: When you update the claim block, please ensure we link to the evidence index page, not directly to internal Jira/Confluence. Marketplace reviewers will need something shareable.

Marcus Lin: Yep. Thanks all. (reaction: :white_check_mark: x4)
