`<domain>.<resource>.<action>`
- `domain`: `org`, `project`, `deployment`, `audit_logs`, `billing`, `platform` (?)
- `resource`: snake_case
- `action`: from allowed verbs

Naomi Feldman: Small question: is `audit_logs` a domain or resource? Example `audit_logs.export` has only 2 segments.

Jordan Lee: Good catch. I’d rather keep 3 segments always. So domain `audit`, resource `logs`, action `export` -> `audit.logs.export`.

Marcus Lin: That reads weird but is consistent.

Ethan Park: Alternative: treat “audit_logs” as resource and have domain `org` or `project`? Like `org.audit_logs.export`.

Priya Natarajan: I like `org.audit_logs.export` because audit logs are scoped to org anyway (at least today). And we might have `project.audit_logs.export` later if we add project-level audit streams.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: +1. Also makes scoping clearer in docs (“This role can export org audit logs”).

Jordan Lee: Works for me. Then domain is the scope container, not the service.

Jordan Lee: So:
```
org.audit_logs.list
org.audit_logs.export
org.api_keys.rotate
project.routing_policies.update
deployment.model_pins.update
```

Marcus Lin: That’s pretty solid.

Amara Diallo: For routing, are we sure it’s `project.routing_policies.update` and not `project.routing.update`? “policy” is an overloaded word here.

Priya Natarajan: We should match the actual object name in the control plane: “routing policy” is the thing with fallback + region weights, so `routing_policies` is fine.

Naomi Feldman: Another question: do we include “environment” as a domain or as a scope on the resource? Like `project.environments.update` vs `environment.routing_policies.update`.

Jordan Lee: Naming doesn’t encode scope. Scope is in the evaluation input (org_id/project_id/env_id/cluster_id). Permission string should not vary based on scope.

Jordan Lee: So you can have `project.routing_policies.update` evaluated at env scope depending on request context. (Policy engine checks binding scope).

Marcus Lin: Right, scope semantics are separate.

Brian Murphy: For docs, we should include a “permission naming rules” section + a “how to request a new permission” template. Otherwise every team will invent new patterns.

Marcus Lin: Action items:
- Jordan: update ADR-014 draft with:
  - dot-separated, lowercase, snake_case segments
  - no service names in permission IDs
  - no version suffix in string; versioning via metadata + deprecation policy
  - verb set + guidance against `manage`
- Naomi: sanity-check against customer-facing terminology + console UX (permission viewer/search)
- Amara/Ethan: confirm gateway + control plane annotations can support `org.*` / `project.*` patterns cleanly

Priya Natarajan: +1. Also add lint rules in the permission registry PR so we fail CI on format violations.

Jordan Lee: On it. I’ll drop a PR comment in `pr-28134` once I’ve updated the linter rules.

Amara Diallo: :thumbsup: Also please reserve `internal.*` or `platform.*` now, so we don’t end up with random “ops_*” strings later.

Marcus Lin: Good call. Let’s reserve `platform.*` for operator-only actions.

Naomi Feldman: Works. One more: should we prefix everything with `redwood.`?

Jordan Lee: I don’t think so. Adds noise, no extra value. These aren’t globally unique identifiers; they’re scoped to Redwood policy bundle.

Priya Natarajan: Agree. Keep short.

Marcus Lin: Consensus seems: no `redwood.` prefix.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: +1. In UI the shorter the better.

Marcus Lin: Cool. I’ll mark this thread as the input to ADR-014 and link it in Confluence. Thanks all.

source.content:
Aisha Rahman: Need quick approval: Northstar Bank (Dedicated) wants *no prompt/completion logging* + metadata only for 30d. Blocking go-live. Any issues?

Priya Natarajan: Approved. Set payload retention to 0 (dont persist prompts/completions). Metadata retention 30d is fine. Please record in INT ticket + exception register. :thumbsup:

Alex Martinez: Cool. Ill implement via log pipeline drop + TTL override. Will post verification.

Hannah Schmitt: +1. Make sure customer acceptance is captured (email) and link it in INT-1842.

source.thread:
Marcus Lin: We need to lock the exception process for any temporarily uncovered sensitive actions (aka waivers). Also: how are we tracking expirations in Linear so we don’t end up with “temporary” gaps forever?

Naomi Feldman: +1. This is the last open question for launch gating. I’m fine with waivers, but only if they’re timeboxed and visible.

Olga Petrov: From a SOC2 evidence POV: we need a defined approval workflow, documented rationale, compensating controls, and an expiry + re-review cadence. Auditors will ask “show me the exceptions and who approved them.”

Talia Benson: Proposal: use Linear as the system of record. Create a dedicated issue template: “Audit logging exception / waiver”. Use Due Date as expiry date. Add labels: `audit-logging`, `waiver`.

Sahana Iyer: Works for CI too. If we reference the Linear ticket ID in the code allowlist, we can fail builds once due date passes (or at least alert).

Logan Wright: For dashboards, I can treat “expected but waived” as a separate bucket so we don’t mask the gap. Something like: Expected = Observed + Waived + Missing.

Ethan Park: What’s the approval chain? I’d like to avoid random “LGTM” in a comment being the only sign-off.

Marcus Lin: Minimum bar I want:
- Waiver must be in Linear (no Slack-only).
- Must include: action surface, expected event_type(s), why not covered, owner, and remediation plan.
- Must be timeboxed.
- Approval from Security Eng + GRC for anything customer-facing.

Naomi Feldman: Can we define default/max durations? Like 30d default; >30d requires extra approval.

Olga Petrov: +1. Also: if a waiver is granted, we should capture “compensating control” (e.g., alternative logs, manual reviews, access restrictions).

Kevin Osei: For exporter/SIEM stuff, we’ve had cases where “not possible to log” is really “pipeline not reliable.” Exceptions should say whether the gap is producer-side vs delivery-side.

Talia Benson: Linear template fields (in description):
- **Sensitive action(s):**
- **Service / component:**
- **Expected audit event_type:**
- **Gap type:** producer missing / schema mismatch / delivery risk
- **Customer impact:** hosted/dedicated/private + which tenants (if known)
- **Compensating controls:**
- **Owner:**
- **Expiry (Due Date):**
- **Remediation ticket(s):** link to ENG/PM tasks
- **Approvers:** Security Eng + GRC (+ Eng lead if >30d)

Sahana Iyer: For code enforcement: we can add `audit_waivers.yaml` in repo with entries like:
- linear_id
- expires_at
- event_type(s) waived
- code_owner/team
Lint can check `expires_at` and fail after expiry.

Marcus Lin: That’s good. I want “fail after expiry” (hard gate), but we can start with warnings for 1 week while teams migrate.

Ethan Park: What about private installer where operator identity can be missing (airgapped / no SSO)? Is that a waiver or a separate schema requirement?

Olga Petrov: That’s not a waiver if it’s by design and documented. If we *can’t* capture identity, we document actor as `type=system` / `type=operator` with whatever identifiers are available, and note the limitation. Waivers are for missing events / missing coverage.

Naomi Feldman: Agree. Waiver should be “sensitive action happening without an audit event” or “event emitted but non-compliant with required fields.”

Logan Wright: I’ll need the Linear labels standardized so I can pull them into the coverage report. If we go with `waiver`, I can build a nightly sync that checks due dates and posts in #eng-security when something is expiring.

Talia Benson: I’ll set up a saved Linear view: Label contains `waiver` AND Project = “Audit Logging v1” AND Due Date in next 14 days.

Kevin Osei: Can we also require a link to a customer ticket if it came from support (SUP-xxxxx) so we can close the loop?

Marcus Lin: Yes, add “Related Support/Customer refs” field. Keep it optional but recommended.

Sahana Iyer: One more: where do we put the “approval” in Linear? Linear doesn’t have a formal approval object.

Olga Petrov: Put it in the issue as a checklist:
- [ ] Security Eng approval (name + date)
- [ ] GRC approval (name + date)
- [ ] Eng owner approval (if >30d)
And link to the Slack thread if needed.

Naomi Feldman: Also add a requirement that waiver issues can’t be closed unless remediation ticket is in “Done” OR a new waiver is filed (with new due date). Avoid “close and forget.”

Marcus Lin: Guardrails:
- Default expiry: 30 days.
- Max expiry: 90 days.
- >30 days requires my approval (or Priya if I’m out) + GRC.
- Anything that affects Private regulated customers: always loop Olga.

Logan Wright: :thumbsup: I’ll mark waived actions in the dashboard so the “100% coverage” claim can be backed by “100% covered or explicitly waived with expiry.”

Talia Benson: I’ll draft the Linear template + saved view today and drop the link here.

Sahana Iyer: I’ll add a repo doc snippet + propose the `audit_waivers.yaml` format in ENG-4831 follow-up. We can wire it into the existing lint pipeline.

Kevin Osei: I’ll make sure exporter normalization doesn’t accidentally “hide” waived producers (e.g., drops invalid events). We should still count them as missing/waived.

Marcus Lin: Great. Let’s document this in the exception process Confluence page too, but Linear is the source of truth. Talia please include that in the template description.

Talia Benson: will do. :white_check_mark:

Olga Petrov: Once it’s in place, I’ll add a line in the evidence mapping: “Audit logging exceptions are tracked in Linear with approvals and expirations; report exported for audits.”

Naomi Feldman: +1. This unblocks the launch gating wording on my side.

Marcus Lin: Cool. Please link the template + saved view when ready, then we’ll post in #eng-platform so service owners know how to request waivers.

source.messages:
Hanae Suzuki: final push before we publish the Confluence docs + ADRs for audit logging. can folks do a last review pass today/tomorrow? dropping checklist + who i think should sign off.

Hanae Suzuki: ✅ Final review checklist (please reply w/ :thumbsup: when done)
1) Confluence pages present + consistent
- audit-log-schema-v1.json page: required fields, examples, JSON schema link works
- sensitive-actions-event-taxonomy.json page: event_type naming consistent w/ registry
- audit-log-coverage-definition.json page: “100% coverage” definition + exclusions process
- audit-log-versioning-and-backcompat-policy.json page: v1 → v1.x rules, deprecation window
- audit-logging-redaction-and-data-classification.json page: no secrets/PII guidance, hashing rules
- audit-logging-control-mapping-soc2-iso.json page: control IDs + evidence examples
- audit-log-events-logging-standard.json page: eng standard + failure semantics
- ADRs: adr-audit-log-schema-and-event-taxonomy + adr-audit-event-delivery-guarantees

2) Cross-links + nav
- each page links to the others (esp schema ↔ taxonomy ↔ logging standard)
- ADR pages link back to Confluence standards
- runbooks referenced where applicable (pipeline incident, coverage regression)

3) Content sanity
- “actor/target/resource” terminology consistent everywhere
- schema_version language consistent (no “vNext” / “TBD” left behind)
- no customer-facing promises on delivery semantics beyond what we can back (at-least-once vs best-effort wording)

4) Examples validate
- example events conform to schema (ideally run through validator)
- examples do NOT contain: API keys, tokens, prompt text, email addresses (unless explicitly allowed + hashed)

5) Cardinality + field constraints called out
- any free-form strings have guidance (diff_summary is capped, user_agent capped, etc)
- enumerations for event_type + action + outcome documented

Proposed reviewers / sign-off:
- Schema + redaction: @Marcus Lin @Hanae Suzuki
- Observability standard + delivery wording: @Allison Grant
- Control mapping / evidence: @Olga Petrov
- SIEM/export guidance references: @Kevin Osei
- Coverage/dashboard language: @Logan Wright
- Editorial pass / doc quality: @Elliot Price
- Product wording for external shareables: @Naomi Feldman

Timeline target:
- Review complete by Thu 2pm PT
- Publish to Confluence Thu EOD
- Announce in #eng + #security-compliance Fri AM

Marcus Lin: +1. thanks Hanae. for schema/redaction: i’ll focus on “don’t log secrets” + actor/impersonation bits. pls flag anything that sounds like we’re guaranteeing 0-drop.

Allison Grant: i can take delivery wording + logging standard. quick q: are we explicitly saying “audit event emission is non-blocking” for admin workflows? that was the last debate.

Hanae Suzuki: yeah, currently says “non-blocking; buffered best-effort; critical paths should continue even if sink degraded” but also references at-least-once downstream when ingestion is healthy. would love your eyes on that section.

Olga Petrov: i’ll review the SOC2/ISO mapping page. can you confirm which screenshots/queries we’re comfortable committing to for evidence? last time auditors asked for exact query examples.

Hanae Suzuki: yep—page has “sample queries (non-binding)” and separates “required evidence” vs “suggested artifacts.” but please tweak if too hand-wavy.

Kevin Osei: i’ll check exporter bits + anywhere we mention Splunk/Sentinel. also want to ensure we instruct customers to de-dupe by event_id (and not request_id).

Logan Wright: on coverage language: i’ll verify the “expected vs observed event types” section lines up with what ENG-4828 is building. we should avoid implying realtime completeness checks if the dashboard is delayed.

Elliot Price: i can do an editorial pass + link hygiene. do we have a standard page label/tag set? (so folks can find these later)

Hanae Suzuki: good call. i tagged with: “security”, “compliance”, “audit-logging”, “observability-standards”. if there’s a canonical label list, i’ll align.

Sahana Iyer: i’m not on the official reviewer list but i can skim for CI/lint mentions. i saw one section referencing “build-time” lints—just want to ensure it doesn’t promise it’s already enforced everywhere.

Hanae Suzuki: please do 🙏. there’s a sentence like “CI will fail for unregistered event_type” which is true for new producers but not for legacy yet. we should tighten wording.

Naomi Feldman: i’ll review anything that could end up in external security FAQ / customer packets. specifically: retention statements, export targets, and “coverage” claims. please avoid “100% today” language unless scoped to “sensitive actions defined in taxonomy v1”.

Vivek Kulkarni: can i sanity-check Private parity statements? we’ve had customers interpret “same as hosted” as “all features day 0”. i’ll look for wording like that.

Hanae Suzuki: yes pls. Private sections are intentionally cautious but would love your stamp.

Marcus Lin: also: can we add a small callout on duplicates? retries => duplicates are expected; consumer should de-dupe on event_id. support keeps getting this question.

Kevin Osei: +1. i’ll add a short note in versioning/backcompat policy + exporter section: “at-least-once delivery; duplicates possible; use event_id for idempotency”.

Allison Grant: reviewed logging standard doc. changes requested:
- replace “guaranteed delivery” w/ “target delivery SLO”
- add explicit behavior during sink outage: queue up to N, then drop w/ metric + alert (don’t block request)
- add note: event_time vs observed_time semantics

Hanae Suzuki: ack. can you paste exact wording you want for the “sink outage” paragraph? i’ll incorporate.

Allison Grant: suggested text:
“Services MUST NOT block customer/admin requests on audit sink availability. Producers SHOULD buffer audit events locally up to a bounded limit; on overflow they MUST increment an ‘audit_event_dropped_total’ metric and include ‘audit_delivery_status=degraded’ in logs/telemetry. The platform provides at-least-once delivery when the pipeline is healthy; duplicates may occur.”

Hanae Suzuki: perfect, applying.

Olga Petrov: control mapping review notes:
- add explicit owner names for evidence collection (Security Eng vs SRE)
- for SOC2 CC7.2/CC7.3, include example “investigation trail” fields (event_id, actor_id, trace_id)
- keep query examples but label as “illustrative” (agree)

Hanae Suzuki: thanks. will update owners section and add the correlation field callout.

Elliot Price: editorial pass done. fixes:
- 3 broken links (ADR URL had old space key)
- inconsistent capitalization “schema version” vs “schema_version” in prose
- added a short “Terminology” section at top of schema page (actor vs subject vs target)

Hanae Suzuki: merged those edits. :pray:

Sahana Iyer: skimmed for CI/lint. suggest rewording:
From: “CI enforces required audit events for sensitive actions.”
To: “CI enforces schema validity + registered event_type for new/modified producers; coverage is verified via integration tests + telemetry dashboards.”

Hanae Suzuki: +1, updating.

Logan Wright: coverage doc looks good. one tweak: “coverage dashboard updates every 5 minutes” is too specific. we don’t want to lock that in. propose: “near-real-time (minutes) depending on telemetry pipeline.”

Hanae Suzuki: changed.

Naomi Feldman: product pass ok w/ one nit: avoid “SOC2 compliant” phrasing. changed to “supports SOC 2 / ISO-aligned controls” + “used as evidence for audits.”

Vivek Kulkarni: Private wording ok. i changed one sentence: “Private emits equivalent events where operator identity is available; if not available, actor_type=system and operator fields omitted.” avoids promising perfect operator attribution.

Hanae Suzuki: awesome. thanks all.

Hanae Suzuki: ✅ Status check: please react when your section is approved so i can publish.
- Marcus (schema/redaction): ?
- Allison (logging standard/delivery ADR): ?
- Olga (control mapping): ?
- Kevin (export mentions): ?
- Logan (coverage): ?
- Elliot (editorial/links): done
- Naomi (product wording): done
- Vivek (Private parity wording): done

Marcus Lin: :thumbsup: schema/redaction approved after latest edits.

Allison Grant: :thumbsup: approved.

Olga Petrov: :thumbsup: approved.

Kevin Osei: :thumbsup: approved (added dedupe blurb; pls ensure it’s consistent in both exporter + delivery ADR).

Logan Wright: :thumbsup: approved.

Hanae Suzuki: publishing now + will post announcement draft here before i send.

Hanae Suzuki: Announcement draft for #eng / #security-compliance (feedback welcome):
“New audit logging standards are live in Confluence: canonical schema v1, sensitive action taxonomy, coverage definition, versioning/backcompat, and redaction rules, plus ADRs for schema + delivery semantics. These docs define the required fields + event_type registry used across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. Please use the schema library + event_type registry for new producers; reach out in #eng-security for reviews/questions.”

Marcus Lin: looks good. add “do not log secrets” one-liner maybe.

Hanae Suzuki: will add. publishing + sending in the morning.

Marcus Lin: ty everyone. let’s make sure PR reviewers link back to these docs during rollout.

Hanae Suzuki: +1. closing thread.

Hanae Suzuki: final push before we publish the Confluence docs + ADRs for audit logging. can folks do a last review pass today/tomorrow? dropping checklist + who i think should sign off.

1) Confluence pages present + consistent
- audit-log-schema-v1.json page: required fields, examples, JSON schema link works
- sensitive-actions-event-taxonomy.json page: event_type naming consistent w/ registry
- audit-log-coverage-definition.json page: “100% coverage” definition + exclusions process
- audit-log-versioning-and-backcompat-policy.json page: v1 → v1.x rules, deprecation window
- audit-logging-redaction-and-data-classification.json page: no secrets/PII guidance, hashing rules

- audit-log-events-logging-standard.json page: eng standard + failure semantics
- ADRs: adr-audit-log-schema-and-event-taxonomy + adr-audit-event-delivery-guarantees

2) Cross-links + nav
- each page links to the others (esp schema ↔ taxonomy ↔ logging standard)
- ADR pages link back to Confluence standards
- runbooks referenced where applicable (pipeline incident, coverage regression)

3) Content sanity
- “actor/target/resource” terminology consistent everywhere
- schema_version language consistent (no “vNext” / “TBD” left behind)
- no customer-facing promises on delivery semantics beyond what we can back (at-least-once vs best-effort wording)

4) Examples validate
- example events conform to schema (ideally run through validator)
- examples do NOT contain: API keys, tokens, prompt text, email addresses (unless explicitly allowed + hashed)

5) Cardinality + field constraints called out
- any free-form strings have guidance (diff_summary is capped, user_agent capped, etc)
- enumerations for event_type + action + outcome documented

Proposed reviewers / sign-off:
- Schema + redaction: @Marcus Lin @Hanae Suzuki
- Observability standard + delivery wording: @Allison Grant
- Control mapping / evidence: @Olga Petrov
- SIEM/export guidance references: @Kevin Osei
- Coverage/dashboard language: @Logan Wright
- Editorial pass / doc quality: @Elliot Price
- Product wording for external shareables: @Naomi Feldman

Timeline target:
- Review complete by Thu 2pm PT
- Publish to Confluence Thu EOD
- Announce in #eng + #security-compliance Fri AM

Marcus Lin: +1. thanks Hanae. for schema/redaction: i’ll focus on “don’t log secrets” + actor/impersonation bits. pls flag anything that sounds like we’re guaranteeing 0-drop.

Allison Grant: i can take delivery wording + logging standard. quick q: are we explicitly saying “audit event emission is non-blocking” for admin workflows? that was the last debate.

Hanae Suzuki: yeah, currently says “non-blocking; buffered best-effort; critical paths should continue even if sink degraded” but also references at-least-once downstream when ingestion is healthy. would love your eyes on that section.

Olga Petrov: i’ll review the SOC2/ISO mapping page. can you confirm which screenshots/queries we’re comfortable committing to for evidence? last time auditors asked for exact query examples.

Hanae Suzuki: yep—page has “sample queries (non-binding)” and separates “required evidence” vs “suggested artifacts.” but please tweak if too hand-wavy.

Kevin Osei: i’ll check exporter bits + anywhere we mention Splunk/Sentinel. also want to ensure we instruct customers to de-dupe by event_id (and not request_id).

Logan Wright: on coverage language: i’ll verify the “expected vs observed event types” section lines up with what ENG-4828 is building. we should avoid implying realtime completeness checks if the dashboard is delayed.

Elliot Price: i can do an editorial pass + link hygiene. do we have a standard page label/tag set? (so folks can find these later)

Hanae Suzuki: good call. i tagged with: “security”, “compliance”, “audit-logging”, “observability-standards”. if there’s a canonical label list, i’ll align.

Sahana Iyer: i’m not on the official reviewer list but i can skim for CI/lint mentions. i saw one section referencing “build-time” lints—just want to ensure it doesn’t promise it’s already enforced everywhere.

Hanae Suzuki: please do 🙏. there’s a sentence like “CI will fail for unregistered event_type” which is true for new producers but not for legacy yet. we should tighten wording.

Naomi Feldman: i’ll review anything that could end up in external security FAQ / customer packets. specifically: retention statements, export targets, and “coverage” claims. please avoid “100% today” language unless scoped to “sensitive actions defined in taxonomy v1”.

Vivek Kulkarni: can i sanity-check Private parity statements? we’ve had customers interpret “same as hosted” as “all features day 0”. i’ll look for wording like that.

Hanae Suzuki: yes pls. Private sections are intentionally cautious but would love your stamp.

Marcus Lin: also: can we add a small callout on duplicates? retries => duplicates are expected; consumer should de-dupe on event_id. support keeps getting this question.

Kevin Osei: +1. i’ll add a short note in versioning/backcompat policy + exporter section: “at-least-once delivery; duplicates possible; use event_id for idempotency”.

Allison Grant: reviewed logging standard doc. changes requested:
- replace “guaranteed delivery” w/ “target delivery SLO”
- add explicit behavior during sink outage: queue up to N, then drop w/ metric + alert (don’t block request)
- add note: event_time vs observed_time semantics

Hanae Suzuki: ack. can you paste exact wording you want for the “sink outage” paragraph? i’ll incorporate.

Allison Grant: suggested text:
“Services MUST NOT block customer/admin requests on audit sink availability. Producers SHOULD buffer audit events locally up to a bounded limit; on overflow they MUST increment an ‘audit_event_dropped_total’ metric and include ‘audit_delivery_status=degraded’ in logs/telemetry. The platform provides at-least-once delivery when the pipeline is healthy; duplicates may occur.”

Hanae Suzuki: perfect, applying.

Olga Petrov: control mapping review notes:
- add explicit owner names for evidence collection (Security Eng vs SRE)
- for SOC2 CC7.2/CC7.3, include example “investigation trail” fields (event_id, actor_id, trace_id)
- keep query examples but label as “illustrative” (agree)

Hanae Suzuki: thanks. will update owners section and add the correlation field callout.

Elliot Price: editorial pass done. fixes:
- 3 broken links (ADR URL had old space key)
- inconsistent capitalization “schema version” vs “schema_version” in prose
- added a short “Terminology” section at top of schema page (actor vs subject vs target)

Hanae Suzuki: merged those edits. :pray:

Sahana Iyer: skimmed for CI/lint. suggest rewording:
From: “CI enforces required audit events for sensitive actions.”
To: “CI enforces schema validity + registered event_type for new/modified producers; coverage is verified via integration tests + telemetry dashboards.”

Hanae Suzuki: +1, updating.

Logan Wright: coverage doc looks good. one tweak: “coverage dashboard updates every 5 minutes” is too specific. we don’t want to lock that in. propose: “near-real-time (minutes) depending on telemetry pipeline.”

Hanae Suzuki: changed.

Naomi Feldman: product pass ok w/ one nit: avoid “SOC2 compliant” phrasing. changed to “supports SOC 2 / ISO-aligned controls” + “used as evidence for audits.”

Vivek Kulkarni: Private wording ok. i changed one sentence: “Private emits equivalent events where operator identity is available; if not available, actor_type=system and operator fields omitted.” avoids promising perfect operator attribution.

Hanae Suzuki: awesome. thanks all.

Hanae Suzuki: ✅ Status check: please react when your section is approved so i can publish.
- Marcus (schema/redaction): ?
- Allison (logging standard/delivery ADR): ?
- Olga (control mapping): ?
- Kevin (export mentions): ?
- Logan (coverage): ?
- Elliot (editorial/links): done
- Naomi (product wording): done
- Vivek (Private parity wording): done

Marcus Lin: :thumbsup: schema/redaction approved after latest edits.

Allison Grant: :thumbsup: approved.

Olga Petrov: :thumbsup: approved.

Kevin Osei: :thumbsup: approved (added dedupe blurb; pls ensure it’s consistent in both exporter + delivery ADR).

Logan Wright: :thumbsup: approved.

Hanae Suzuki: publishing now + will post announcement draft here before i send.

Hanae Suzuki: Announcement draft for #eng / #security-compliance (feedback welcome):
“New audit logging standards are live in Confluence: canonical schema v1, sensitive action taxonomy, coverage definition, versioning/backcompat, and redaction rules, plus ADRs for schema + delivery semantics. These docs define the required fields + event_type registry used across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. Please use the schema library + event_type registry for new producers; reach out in #eng-security for reviews/questions.”

Marcus Lin: looks good. add “do not log secrets” one-liner maybe.

Hanae Suzuki: will add. publishing + sending in the morning.

Marcus Lin: ty everyone. let’s make sure PR reviewers link back to these docs during rollout.

Hanae Suzuki: +1. closing thread.

source.body:
Marcus Lin: Kicking off security review thread for Hosted data residency enforcement. Need to close threat model Qs + audit event schema + confirm approvers for GA. Links:
- PRD: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/hosted-residency-prd
- ADR-021 boundaries: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/adr-021
- ADR-022 error contract: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/adr-022

Naomi Feldman: thx Marcus. aiming to freeze the policy model + error contract copy this week. what do you need from us to get security sign-off? 

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Two buckets:
1) Threat model answers (where content can flow, including logs/traces).
2) Evidence for controls + approvals list for GA.
Also confirm we’re not accidentally allowing cross-region “fallback” in any failure mode.

Jordan Lee: On (2) I can share policy engine evaluation flow + where we attach decision context to downstream calls. But can someone clarify what you mean by “content” for audit/logging? We currently treat prompt+completion as content; token counts as metadata.

Marcus Lin: yep, content definition is key. From ADR-021 draft: “customer content” = prompt, tool args, retrieved docs, completion, embeddings input/output. Metadata allowed cross-region: request_id, org_id, project_id, model_id, region_id, timing, token counts, status code, error_code.
Question: do we ever emit prompt snippets in routing/gateway logs today?

Amara Diallo: gateway: we log structured fields; request/response bodies are not logged. BUT we sometimes include `error.detail` from downstream which can contain user text (ugh). We’ve been trying to scrub.

Brian Murphy: +1. For new residency errors we’re proposing deterministic errors built at gateway, so no downstream error detail. For non-residency errors, we still pass through sanitized message. We should confirm scrubbers run before log export.

Logan Wright: telemetry pipeline: for hosted prod, we have a “no payloads” guarantee, but traces can contain attributes. We’ve been removing `prompt` / `completion` attrs, but I want to make sure policy decision context doesn’t accidentally include raw input.

Sean Gallagher: SRE perspective: enforcement blocks will spike logs, and support wants a breadcrumb. We need to ensure the breadcrumb is safe and still useful.

Marcus Lin: Threat model questions to answer (please reply inline):
A) Where is residency evaluated? (control plane vs gateway vs routing)
B) What happens if routing is down / policy svc timeouts?
C) Any async/background jobs touching request content (evals, caching, abuse detection) that might be cross-region?
D) Audit logs: what fields + where stored + retention per region.

Jordan Lee: A) evaluation happens in routing + gateway. Routing evaluates “allowed_regions” and chooses region; gateway enforces request header/endpoint region mismatch and attaches `residency_context` for downstream.
We also have control plane policy service as source-of-truth config, but not in the hot path beyond caching.

Benji Okafor: Adding detail: routing calls PolicyCache (local) which refreshes from policy service. The evaluation function returns:
- `policy_scope` (org|project|api_key)
- `policy_id` (uuid)
- `allowed_regions` (set)
- `primary_region`
- `decision` (allow|block)
- `selected_region` (if allow)
- `block_reason` (enum)
No prompt content involved.

Amara Diallo: B) if policy cache missing/expired AND policy service times out: current proposal is fail-closed *only* when residency feature flag is enabled for that org. Else normal behavior.
We need explicit sign-off on fail-closed vs fail-open for GA.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: For orgs who have configured residency, fail-closed is the only acceptable stance. For orgs w/o residency configured, fail-open is fine.
But we need clarity: do we have a “default policy” created on migration that sets allowed_regions = ALL? If yes, that turns into “configured” and would flip fail-closed globally.

Benji Okafor: migration plan: no default policy objects. Residency fields null unless explicitly set. So “configured” = non-null allowed_regions OR primary_region set.

Naomi Feldman: Product agrees. We’ll keep it opt-in (and likely Enterprise-only at first).

Olga Petrov: Please document that in the controls narrative: “fail-closed when customer config indicates residency requirement; otherwise standard routing.” Auditors will ask.

Logan Wright: C) async jobs: we have abuse detection signals and eval sampling in hosted. For residency-pinned orgs, we should ensure sampling is disabled or processed in-region.
Currently, abuse detection pipeline aggregates globally (US). That’s metadata only (no prompts), but need to confirm.

Marcus Lin: The boundary doc says abuse detection can process metadata cross-region, but NOT content. Confirm “metadata only” is actually true.

Logan Wright: For hosted we send: request_id, org_id, model_id, token counts, response codes, latency histograms. No payload. We do have an optional “debug sampling” that captures prompt snippets in staging only. Not enabled in prod.

Rafael Mendes: Worth adding a guardrail: if org has residency policy, we hard-disable any sampling toggles even if someone flips a debug flag.

Chloe Martin: Console side: “blocked requests” view will show:
- timestamp
- request_id
- endpoint
- attempted_region
- allowed_regions
- block_reason
No content. We should also include a “copy for support” snippet that doesn’t leak content.

Brian Murphy: For error contract (ADR-022): We’re using HTTP 409 with error payload:
`{"error":{"type":"residency_violation","code":"RESIDENCY_REGION_NOT_ALLOWED","message":"...","request_id":"...","help_url":"..."}}`
Open question: include `policy_id`? Security had concerns about enumeration.

Marcus Lin: I lean NO on policy_id in public response. request_id is enough; support can map internally.
But we can include `policy_scope` (org/project/api_key) as that’s not sensitive.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Agree: do not expose internal identifiers unless we have a clear customer need. request_id + help_url + “where to change settings” is fine.

Naomi Feldman: ok we’ll adjust. Console can show policy_id internally.

Olga Petrov: D) Audit logs: need event schema with required fields + examples. Proposed events?

Logan Wright: Draft event names:
- `residency.policy.created`
- `residency.policy.updated`
- `residency.policy.deleted`
- `residency.policy.override_enabled` / `override_disabled`
Fields (all strings): `event_id`, `ts`, `actor_type` (user|service), `actor_id`, `org_id`, `project_id` (optional), `api_key_id` (optional), `policy_scope`, `policy_id`, `before`/`after` diff (careful: diff as JSON would be nested; we can store as stringified JSON), `source_ip`, `request_id` (if from API).

Marcus Lin: Keep “diff” minimal and ensure it contains only config fields (regions, primary). No content risk.
Also add `reason` for overrides + required approver list.

Sean Gallagher: For overrides: SRE will want a break-glass path when false positives block legit traffic. But it needs approvals + expiry.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Break-glass requirements:
- time-bound (max 1h) and scoped (org/project)
- requires on-call + security approval (or pre-approved runbook)
- always emits audit event + pager annotation

Rafael Mendes: We can implement override as feature flag with TTL. But we’ll need to ensure it doesn’t allow cross-region processing for content, it should only allow requests when the reason is “policy service unreachable” etc. If it bypasses allowed_regions, that violates the promise.

Jordan Lee: Agree. override should be “fail-open on eval failure” NOT “ignore allowed_regions.” Two different toggles.

Marcus Lin: +1. Let’s define override types:
1) `evaluation_fail_open` (only if policy service/cache issues)
2) `enforcement_bypass` (NOT allowed for GA)

Naomi Feldman: good call. We’ll make #2 not exist in product.

SecurityBot: Reminder: Please file final threat model doc in the Security Review folder before requesting CISO sign-off. Template: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/security-threat-model-template

Marcus Lin: Approvals needed for GA (proposal):
- Security Eng (me) final review ✅
- CISO (Aisha) sign-off
- GRC (Olga) evidence pack v1 ready
- SRE readiness (Sean/Rafael): dashboards + runbook + paging thresholds
- Product (Naomi): customer comms + docs ready
Anything missing?

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Add: “data flow diagram” attached to threat model + validation that subprocessors are region-aligned. Even if out-of-scope, auditors ask.

Olga Petrov: And: change-management artifacts (ADRs, PR links) referenced in evidence pack. Also screenshot/links of Console settings page once ready.

Amara Diallo: For DFD: gateway->routing->runtime are all in-region. Control plane is global, but only config metadata. We should explicitly call out which services are regionalized.

Logan Wright: For log storage: we have per-region log sinks for hosted (EU->EU). But audit logs are in control plane DB (global). We need to confirm audit logs do NOT include content, so global storage is acceptable.

Marcus Lin: Exactly. We should write it: “audit logs may be stored globally; they contain configuration and actor metadata only.” That aligns with our current policy.

Brian Murphy: On error messages: copy currently says “This request was routed to us-east-1 but your project is pinned to eu-west-1.” Is it ok to reveal region names? I think yes.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Region names are fine. Don’t include internal cluster IDs.

Sean Gallagher: Monitoring: we’ll alert on `residency_block_rate` anomalies. Need a “known rollout spike” window so we don’t page during initial enablement.

Rafael Mendes: We can gate paging behind “policy enabled for >30m” per org, and global alert thresholds during rollout.

Marcus Lin: Action items:
- Jordan/Benji: answer B/C in threat model doc, specifically eval failure modes + any async systems
- Logan: finalize audit event schema + confirm per-region log sinks and trace attrs scrubbed
- Amara/Brian: ensure streaming path returns standardized 409 w/ code + no generic 500 (and no downstream detail leakage)
- Sean/Rafael: draft break-glass runbook with override type constraints
- Olga: list evidence pack required artifacts + where to link dashboards

Naomi Feldman: I’ll ping docs team re: public FAQ language (what’s guaranteed vs not). Also will update PRD to reflect fail-closed behavior when configured.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Timeline: I can review final threat model + evidence checklist by EOD Thu. If gaps remain, GA slips. No exceptions.

Marcus Lin: acknowledged. I’ll schedule 30m checkpoint tomorrow and drop the updated threat model link here.

Logan Wright: Quick note: for “residency decision logs” we’re planning a dedicated event `residency.enforcement.decision` emitted in-region. Fields: request_id, org_id, selected_region, allowed_regions, decision, block_reason, policy_scope. No policy_id? (internal only?)

Marcus Lin: internal telemetry can include policy_id if it stays in-region and access controlled. But if it goes to central systems, keep it out.

Logan Wright: got it. we’ll keep `policy_id` only in the regional sink + redact from any global aggregations.

Olga Petrov: Please document that split in evidence pack. Auditors love “regional sink” language but will ask who can access.

Sean Gallagher: Access = SRE + Security only? Or support too?

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Support can access via Console troubleshooting view (already sanitized). Raw telemetry access limited to SRE/Sec. Add to RBAC doc.

Marcus Lin: :thumbsup: closing loop here once docs are updated.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Timeline: I can review final threat model + evidence checklist by EOD Thu. If gaps remain, GA slips. No exceptions.

Marcus Lin: acknowledged. I’ll schedule 30m checkpoint tomorrow and drop the updated threat model link here.

Logan Wright: Quick note: for “residency decision logs” we’re planning a dedicated event `residency.enforcement.decision` emitted in-region. Fields: request_id, org_id, selected_region, allowed_regions, decision, block_reason, policy_scope. No policy_id? (internal only?)

Marcus Lin: internal telemetry can include policy_id if it stays in-region and access controlled. But if it goes to central systems, keep it out.

Logan Wright: got it. we’ll keep `policy_id` only in the regional sink + redact from any global aggregations.

Olga Petrov: Please document that split in evidence pack. Auditors love “regional sink” language but will ask who can access.

Sean Gallagher: Access = SRE + Security only? Or support too?

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Support can access via Console troubleshooting view (already sanitized). Raw telemetry access limited to SRE/Sec. Add to RBAC doc.

Marcus Lin: :thumbsup: closing loop here once docs are updated.
text:
Marcus Lin: Kicking off security review thread for Hosted data residency enforcement. Need to close threat model Qs + audit event schema + confirm approvers for GA. Links:
- PRD: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/hosted-residency-prd
- ADR-021 boundaries: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/adr-021
- ADR-022 error contract: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/adr-022

Naomi Feldman: thx Marcus. aiming to freeze the policy model + error contract copy this week. what do you need from us to get security sign-off? 

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Two buckets:
1) Threat model answers (where content can flow, including logs/traces).
2) Evidence for controls + approvals list for GA.
Also confirm we’re not accidentally allowing cross-region “fallback” in any failure mode.

Jordan Lee: On (2) I can share policy engine evaluation flow + where we attach decision context to downstream calls. But can someone clarify what you mean by “content” for audit/logging? We currently treat prompt+completion as content; token counts as metadata.

Marcus Lin: yep, content definition is key. From ADR-021 draft: “customer content” = prompt, tool args, retrieved docs, completion, embeddings input/output. Metadata allowed cross-region: request_id, org_id, project_id, model_id, region_id, timing, token counts, status code, error_code.
Question: do we ever emit prompt snippets in routing/gateway logs today?

Amara Diallo: gateway: we log structured fields; request/response bodies are not logged. BUT we sometimes include `error.detail` from downstream which can contain user text (ugh). We’ve been trying to scrub.

Brian Murphy: +1. For new residency errors we’re proposing deterministic errors built at gateway, so no downstream error detail. For non-residency errors, we still pass through sanitized message. We should confirm scrubbers run before log export.

Logan Wright: telemetry pipeline: for hosted prod, we have a “no payloads” guarantee, but traces can contain attributes. We’ve been removing `prompt` / `completion` attrs, but I want to make sure policy decision context doesn’t accidentally include raw input.

Sean Gallagher: SRE perspective: enforcement blocks will spike logs, and support wants a breadcrumb. We need to ensure the breadcrumb is safe and still useful.

Marcus Lin: Threat model questions to answer (please reply inline):
A) Where is residency evaluated? (control plane vs gateway vs routing)
B) What happens if routing is down / policy svc timeouts?
C) Any async/background jobs touching request content (evals, caching, abuse detection) that might be cross-region?
D) Audit logs: what fields + where stored + retention per region.

Jordan Lee: A) evaluation happens in routing + gateway. Routing evaluates “allowed_regions” and chooses region; gateway enforces request header/endpoint region mismatch and attaches `residency_context` for downstream.
We also have control plane policy service as source-of-truth config, but not in the hot path beyond caching.

Benji Okafor: Adding detail: routing calls PolicyCache (local) which refreshes from policy service. The evaluation function returns:
- `policy_scope` (org|project|api_key)
- `policy_id` (uuid)
- `allowed_regions` (set)
- `primary_region`
- `decision` (allow|block)
- `selected_region` (if allow)
- `block_reason` (enum)
No prompt content involved.

Amara Diallo: B) if policy cache missing/expired AND policy service times out: current proposal is fail-closed *only* when residency feature flag is enabled for that org. Else normal behavior.
We need explicit sign-off on fail-closed vs fail-open for GA.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: For orgs who have configured residency, fail-closed is the only acceptable stance. For orgs w/o residency configured, fail-open is fine.
But we need clarity: do we have a “default policy” created on migration that sets allowed_regions = ALL? If yes, that turns into “configured” and would flip fail-closed globally.

Benji Okafor: migration plan: no default policy objects. Residency fields null unless explicitly set. So “configured” = non-null allowed_regions OR primary_region set.

Naomi Feldman: Product agrees. We’ll keep it opt-in (and likely Enterprise-only at first).

Olga Petrov: Please document that in the controls narrative: “fail-closed when customer config indicates residency requirement; otherwise standard routing.” Auditors will ask.

Logan Wright: C) async jobs: we have abuse detection signals and eval sampling in hosted. For residency-pinned orgs, we should ensure sampling is disabled or processed in-region.
Currently, abuse detection pipeline aggregates globally (US). That’s metadata only (no prompts), but need to confirm.

Marcus Lin: The boundary doc says abuse detection can process metadata cross-region, but NOT content. Confirm “metadata only” is actually true.

Logan Wright: For hosted we send: request_id, org_id, model_id, token counts, response codes, latency histograms. No payload. We do have an optional “debug sampling” that captures prompt snippets in staging only. Not enabled in prod.

Rafael Mendes: Worth adding a guardrail: if org has residency policy, we hard-disable any sampling toggles even if someone flips a debug flag.

Chloe Martin: Console side: “blocked requests” view will show:
- timestamp
- request_id
- endpoint
- attempted_region
- allowed_regions
- block_reason
No content. We should also include a “copy for support” snippet that doesn’t leak content.

Brian Murphy: For error contract (ADR-022): We’re using HTTP 409 with error payload:
`{"error":{"type":"residency_violation","code":"RESIDENCY_REGION_NOT_ALLOWED","message":"...","request_id":"...","help_url":"..."}}`
Open question: include `policy_id`? Security had concerns about enumeration.

Marcus Lin: I lean NO on policy_id in public response. request_id is enough; support can map internally.
But we can include `policy_scope` (org/project/api_key) as that’s not sensitive.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Agree: do not expose internal identifiers unless we have a clear customer need. request_id + help_url + “where to change settings” is fine.

Naomi Feldman: ok we’ll adjust. Console can show policy_id internally.

Olga Petrov: D) Audit logs: need event schema with required fields + examples. Proposed events?

Logan Wright: Draft event names:
- `residency.policy.created`
- `residency.policy.updated`
- `residency.policy.deleted`
- `residency.policy.override_enabled` / `override_disabled`
Fields (all strings): `event_id`, `ts`, `actor_type` (user|service), `actor_id`, `org_id`, `project_id` (optional), `api_key_id` (optional), `policy_scope`, `policy_id`, `before`/`after` diff (careful: diff as JSON would be nested; we can store as stringified JSON), `source_ip`, `request_id` (if from API).

Marcus Lin: Keep “diff” minimal and ensure it contains only config fields (regions, primary). No content risk.
Also add `reason` for overrides + required approver list.

Sean Gallagher: For overrides: SRE will want a break-glass path when false positives block legit traffic. But it needs approvals + expiry.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Break-glass requirements:
- time-bound (max 1h) and scoped (org/project)
- requires on-call + security approval (or pre-approved runbook)
- always emits audit event + pager annotation

Rafael Mendes: We can implement override as feature flag with TTL. But we’ll need to ensure it doesn’t allow cross-region processing for content, it should only allow requests when the reason is “policy service unreachable” etc. If it bypasses allowed_regions, that violates the promise.

Jordan Lee: Agree. override should be “fail-open on eval failure” NOT “ignore allowed_regions.” Two different toggles.

Marcus Lin: +1. Let’s define override types:
1) `evaluation_fail_open` (only if policy service/cache issues)
2) `enforcement_bypass` (NOT allowed for GA)

Naomi Feldman: good call. We’ll make #2 not exist in product.

SecurityBot: Reminder: Please file final threat model doc in the Security Review folder before requesting CISO sign-off. Template: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/security-threat-model-template

Marcus Lin: Approvals needed for GA (proposal):
- Security Eng (me) final review ✅
- CISO (Aisha) sign-off
- GRC (Olga) evidence pack v1 ready
- SRE readiness (Sean/Rafael): dashboards + runbook + paging thresholds
- Product (Naomi): customer comms + docs ready
Anything missing?

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Add: “data flow diagram” attached to threat model + validation that subprocessors are region-aligned. Even if out-of-scope, auditors ask.

Olga Petrov: And: change-management artifacts (ADRs, PR links) referenced in evidence pack. Also screenshot/links of Console settings page once ready.

Amara Diallo: For DFD: gateway->routing->runtime are all in-region. Control plane is global, but only config metadata. We should explicitly call out which services are regionalized.

Logan Wright: For log storage: we have per-region log sinks for hosted (EU->EU). But audit logs are in control plane DB (global). We need to confirm audit logs do NOT include content, so global storage is acceptable.

Marcus Lin: Exactly. We should write it: “audit logs may be stored globally; they contain configuration and actor metadata only.” That aligns with our current policy.

Brian Murphy: On error messages: copy currently says “This request was routed to us-east-1 but your project is pinned to eu-west-1.” Is it ok to reveal region names? I think yes.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Region names are fine. Don’t include internal cluster IDs.

Sean Gallagher: Monitoring: we’ll alert on `residency_block_rate` anomalies. Need a “known rollout spike” window so we don’t page during initial enablement.

Rafael Mendes: We can gate paging behind “policy enabled for >30m” per org, and global alert thresholds during rollout.

Marcus Lin: Action items:
- Jordan/Benji: answer B/C in threat model doc, specifically eval failure modes + any async systems
- Logan: finalize audit event schema + confirm per-region log sinks and trace attrs scrubbed
- Amara/Brian: ensure streaming path returns standardized 409 w/ code + no generic 500 (and no downstream detail leakage)
- Sean/Rafael: draft break-glass runbook with override type constraints
- Olga: list evidence pack required artifacts + where to link dashboards

Naomi Feldman: I’ll ping docs team re: public FAQ language (what’s guaranteed vs not). Also will update PRD to reflect fail-closed behavior when configured.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Timeline: I can review final threat model + evidence checklist by EOD Thu. If gaps remain, GA slips. No exceptions.

Marcus Lin: acknowledged. I’ll schedule 30m checkpoint tomorrow and drop the updated threat model link here.

Logan Wright: Quick note: for “residency decision logs” we’re planning a dedicated event `residency.enforcement.decision` emitted in-region. Fields: request_id, org_id, selected_region, allowed_regions, decision, block_reason, policy_scope. No policy_id? (internal only?)

Marcus Lin: internal telemetry can include policy_id if it stays in-region and access controlled. But if it goes to central systems, keep it out.

Logan Wright: got it. we’ll keep `policy_id` only in the regional sink + redact from any global aggregations.

Olga Petrov: Please document that split in evidence pack. Auditors love “regional sink” language but will ask who can access.

Sean Gallagher: Access = SRE + Security only? Or support too?

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Support can access via Console troubleshooting view (already sanitized). Raw telemetry access limited to SRE/Sec. Add to RBAC doc.

Marcus Lin: :thumbsup: closing loop here once docs are updated.

source.body:
Marcus Lin: Need a crisp call for the backup/restore toolkit: do we *ever* allow backup artifacts to include secret material (k8s Secrets / DB passwords / API keys), or do we strictly do references only (Vault path, Secrets Manager ARN, etc.)?

Marcus Lin: Context: installer `backup create` is generating manifest + encrypted blob. If we include raw secrets we’re basically creating a new secret store + retention problem. If we *don’t* include them, restores will fail unless customer has their secret system ready.

Hanae Suzuki: Strong preference: backups should NOT contain secret values. Only references + checks (e.g., “expected secret name X exists”, maybe hash of public cert, etc.).

Hanae Suzuki: For k8s secrets specifically, we can record:
- secret name + namespace
- expected keys (not values)
- owner resource
Then `restore apply` can validate + prompt operator to rehydrate.

Connor O'Brien: +1 on not becoming a secret store. BUT: air-gapped/on-prem customers sometimes literally only use k8s Secrets and have no Vault/HSM/Secrets Manager. If we exclude all secret material, restore into a new cluster means they have to manually recreate everything before Redwood services come up.

Connor O'Brien: Do we want an *explicit* opt-in “include k8s secrets” mode for on-prem? (with scary warnings) Or do we keep v1 strict and document “secrets are out of band”?

Kevin Osei: From compliance/evidence POV, storing secret *values* in backup artifacts expands the scope of what we have to prove (access controls, retention, key rotation, deletion, audit trails). Default must be “no secret material.”

Kevin Osei: If we ever add an opt-in mode, we’ll need:
- envelope encryption w/ customer-managed key where possible
- clear retention guidance (“treat as highest sensitivity”)
- audit logging of who generated/downloaded/imported backup artifact

Sean Gallagher: From SRE/drills: references-only is manageable if we make the restore UX explicit.

Sean Gallagher: E.g. restore preflight should stop early with a checklist:
- KMS/Vault reachable (or skip)
- required secret refs exist
- certs present
Otherwise folks run the restore and then we get a flood of “pods crashlooping” tickets.

Fatima Noor: On-prem perspective: most customers already have a “secrets restore” step as part of cluster bootstrap (even if it’s just reapplying their sealed-secrets repo). If we document it + add validation, it’s fine.

Fatima Noor: I’m worried the opt-in “include secrets” becomes the default ask for PS and then we’re stuck supporting it in weird environments.

Connor O'Brien: Agree. Also signing/offline bundle already has enough moving parts. Adding “backup includes secrets” means we now have to reason about plaintext in temp dirs, log redaction, etc.

Hanae Suzuki: Another gotcha: even if we encrypt the artifact, operators will still decrypt during restore. Need to ensure we never write decrypted secrets to disk unless explicitly required. Hard to guarantee across platforms.

Marcus Lin: OK sounds like consensus: v1 = references only, no secret values. We can consider an opt-in later, but only with explicit flag + separate review.

Marcus Lin: Proposed wording for ADR: “Backups MUST NOT contain secret material (credentials, private keys). Backups MAY contain secret *references* and non-sensitive metadata needed to validate presence. Restore requires operator to rehydrate secrets via their secret management mechanism.”

Marcus Lin: Any objections?

Kevin Osei: Works for me. Add: audit logs are separate; if audit logs include sensitive data, that’s handled via audit-log exporter retention doc, not this artifact.

Sean Gallagher: +1. Please make sure runbook says “if you rotate secrets after backup, restore will not roll them back; it will only validate current refs.”

Connor O'Brien: +1. Also call out that “backup encryption” is not a justification to include secrets by default.

Hanae Suzuki: +1. Also note exceptions: TLS cert *public* material can be included (CA bundle, cert chain) but private keys = no.

Marcus Lin: Great. Updating ADR draft now.

Marcus Lin: Link: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/ADR-027+-+Backup+format+and+encryption (see section “Secret handling”).

Marcus Lin: Please add comments by EOD. :thumbsup:

Connor O'Brien: :thumbsup:

Kevin Osei: :thumbsup:

Hanae Suzuki: :thumbsup:

source.content:
Markus Klein: Heads up: Helio Bank security review tomorrow 10am PT. They want “audit logs + SIEM export + delivery guarantees” in one packet. What docs are we comfortable sharing externally? Also need wording on semantics (duplicates, ordering) + what NOT to promise.

Naomi Feldman: For external share, we should stick to: schema, taxonomy, delivery semantics high-level, and redaction/PII posture. Avoid internal dashboards, internal incident history, and anything that looks like we’re committing dates.

Kevin Osei: +1. Biggest pitfall is them hearing “exactly-once” or “no duplicates”. We should explicitly say at-least-once delivery, duplicates possible, use `event_id` for de-dupe. Also ordering is best-effort only.

Olga Petrov: From GRC angle, we should share something that maps to their control expectations without dragging in SOC2 evidence internals. A short “Audit logging overview” + schema excerpt is usually enough.

Marcus Lin: Agree. Let’s define the “approved external packet” so Markus can send consistently.

Elliot Price: I can compile PDFs / links. What’s the canonical Confluence paths to point at?

Naomi Feldman: Use these (sanitized, no internal comments):
- Audit event schema v1 (field dictionary + examples): `confluence/security-and-compliance/audit-logging/audit-log-schema-v1.json`
- Sensitive actions taxonomy: `confluence/security-and-compliance/audit-logging/sensitive-actions-event-taxonomy.json`
- Versioning/backcompat policy: `confluence/security-and-compliance/audit-logging/audit-log-versioning-and-backcompat-policy.json`
- Redaction + data classification: `confluence/security-and-compliance/audit-logging/audit-logging-redaction-and-data-classification.json`
- Delivery semantics ADR (excerpt only, not the whole ADR w/ internal discussion): `confluence/architecture-and-standards/decision-records/adr-audit-event-delivery-guarantees.json`

Kevin Osei: Re delivery semantics language, suggest we say:
- Delivery: at-least-once from producer -> ingestion -> exporter when configured.
- Duplicates: possible during retries / failover.
- Ordering: not guaranteed across services; within a single producer best-effort.
- Latency: typically near-real-time; may be delayed during downstream outages; buffering/retry.
- Integrity: each event has unique `event_id`; includes `request_id`/`trace_id` when applicable.

Hanae Suzuki: And we should explicitly say: audit logs are tamper-evident in the sense of access control + append-only storage on our side, but we’re not claiming “cryptographic immutability / blockchain”. Customers sometimes ask.

Michael Grant: Also add a clear statement about what we *don’t* log:
- No secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords)
- No request/response payloads for inference prompts by default
- PII minimized; certain identifiers hashed/pseudonymized where possible
We should avoid implying we can guarantee “no PII ever”, but we can state the field-level classification + redaction rules.

Markus Klein: Helpful. Helio also asked about Private deployments: “same events?” How do we phrase parity without overcommitting?

Marcus Lin: Phrase it as: “Hosted/Dedicated/Private share the same canonical schema and event taxonomy. Private emits equivalent events for equivalent actions; some environment-specific events include an `environment`/`deployment_mode` marker.”

Naomi Feldman: And if they press for “100% coverage today”, be careful. Say: “We have a defined sensitive-action taxonomy and we are standardizing instrumentation across services; coverage is enforced via CI/lints and verified via telemetry. For any exceptions, we track them with formal expiry + risk review.” No dates.

Olga Petrov: +1. Also: “Exceptions are time-bound and documented.” That reads well for auditors.

Amina Yusuf: For Private specifically, we can say exporter supports Splunk HEC and Azure Sentinel. But avoid promising every SIEM under the sun.

Kevin Osei: Yep. Approved wording: “Supported targets include Splunk HEC and Azure Sentinel/Log Analytics; other destinations via generic HTTP sink (where applicable).”

Elliot Price: Do we have a customer-facing “SIEM export guide” ready, or do we only share config snippets?

Naomi Feldman: For this call, share a 1-pager summary + minimal config examples. Don’t send raw internal runbooks.

Kevin Osei: Also, we should be upfront about de-dupe. Recommend they de-dupe by `event_id` in SIEM. If they want idempotency keys: we provide `event_id` and stable `actor_id`/`target_id`/`action` fields, but ingestion is at-least-once.

Hanae Suzuki: Another pitfall: “audit logs are guaranteed even if the system is down.” We can’t claim that. Better: “audit emission is designed to be resilient; during audit sink outages, events are buffered and retried; if buffers are exhausted, systems may degrade to protect availability and we alert.”

Marcus Lin: + add “audit pipeline health is monitored and alerted; SRE on-call.” That’s okay to say without sharing thresholds.

Markus Klein: Do we want to mention SLOs (latency/drop rate) or keep it vague?

Olga Petrov: Keep it qualitative unless legal asks. If we quote numbers, that becomes a commitment. We can say “monitored with internal SLOs” and “available under NDA in enterprise security exhibit” if needed.

Michael Grant: Also avoid mentioning retention durations unless we’re sure and consistent with contract. Say “configurable retention in accordance with plan and contractual terms; export to customer SIEM for longer retention.”

Naomi Feldman: Great point. Use: “Audit logs are retained per Redwood policy / customer plan; customers can export to SIEM for extended retention and correlation.” No specific days.

Elliot Price: I’ll assemble an “external share bundle” with:
- Schema v1 + examples
- Taxonomy list
- Versioning/backcompat policy summary
- Redaction/classification summary
- Delivery semantics bullet points (approved language)
and I’ll route to @Naomi Feldman + @Michael Grant for quick review.

Kevin Osei: Can we add one example event showing duplicates/`event_id` and correlation IDs? That tends to click with security teams.

Hanae Suzuki: +1. Example like `rbac.role.updated` with `admin_action_id`, `request_id`, `trace_id`, `source_ip`, `actor_type=user`, `impersonated_by` if present.

Marcus Lin: OK but remind: avoid leaking internal field names that aren’t in schema v1. Make sure it validates.

Markus Klein: Action items I’m hearing:
1) Elliot drafts packet + example event.
2) Naomi approves product/security positioning.
3) Michael sanity-checks privacy/retention wording.
4) Kevin provides SIEM export phrasing + “at-least-once/duplicates” paragraph.

Naomi Feldman: Yes. Also, for “roadmap items to avoid promising”:
- “exactly-once delivery”
- “real-time guarantees”
- “full audit log UI” beyond basic view/export config
- “every SIEM integration”
- Any dates for “100% coverage across all Private workflows”
If they ask, we can say “in active rollout” and offer to follow up with an NDA roadmap discussion.

Amina Yusuf: If they ask about installer/operator identity in Private: we can say “where available, we record operator identity (SSO/user) and source host; in air-gapped installs without IdP integration, operator may be recorded as a local service account.”

Kevin Osei: Good call. Make it clear it’s environment-dependent.

Markus Klein: Perfect. I’ll stick to the approved language and redirect detailed roadmap to Naomi offline. Thanks all.

source.messages:
Marcus Lin: looping in on usage insights export + shareable reports. before we flip beta on for any enterprise accounts, need answers on: (1) who can see cost (2) who can export (3) retention + data residency constraints (4) audit log events/fields. can someone drop current plan?

Dylan Brooks: yep. current implementation sketch: insights page behind feature flag + existing cost permission. export is async job -> signed URL. we have "workspace setting: hide cost" concept in console already but not sure it’s enforced in this path yet.

Naomi Feldman: from product/security reqs: cost visibility must be gated by role AND workspace setting. if workspace hides cost, nobody (except maybe workspace owner?) should be able to view/export. we need to confirm desired override semantics.

Kevin Osei: ^ +1. also re: shareable links: if link is created by someone with access, do we allow anyone with link? (pls no) or require auth + RBAC at view time.

Chloe Martin: backend proposal: all insights endpoints require `cost:read` (or `billing:read` depending on final naming) and check workspace flag. exports require `cost:export`. share links would just be a saved report id; fetch still checks auth.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: RBAC side: we can add a dedicated permission `usage_insights.export` if we don’t want to overload billing perms. but we need a clear mapping to existing roles (Admin/Owner vs Developer). today: Developers can see usage (tokens) but not $ in some workspaces.

Cole Summers: fwiw billing totals correctness folks prefer cost view aligns to invoice visibility. so if a user can view invoices, they can view cost insights. exports feel like a stricter permission though.

Paula Mendes: PRD says “who can see cost” is configurable + defaults conservative for beta. we were leaning: only Owners/Admins can see $ by default, others see usage-only. export should be Owners/Admins only.

Marcus Lin: ok, but we need it explicit in security review doc. can we confirm:
- View $ in console: Owners/Admins + workspace setting `allow_cost_visibility=true`
- Export CSV: Owners/Admins + `allow_exports=true` (?)
- Share link: always requires auth + same perms at time of access

Dylan Brooks: we don’t have `allow_exports` as a separate setting yet. we can add but it might slip beta.

Kevin Osei: if we don’t add a setting, at minimum: export permission must be separate from view permission (so we can later clamp down). and exports must be audited.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: implementable short-term:
- `usage_insights.view_cost` permission for any cost fields
- `usage_insights.export` for export endpoints
- roles: Owner/Admin get both; Developer gets none by default
- workspace boolean `cost_visibility_enabled` gates both

Chloe Martin: + enforce same on query service, not just console. otherwise folks can hit API directly.

Marcus Lin: good. next: retention & residency. where are export files stored and how long?

Dylan Brooks: exports are generated server-side in our analytics worker, stored in object storage, and served via signed URL. proposed TTL: 24h for file + 15m signed URL.

Kevin Osei: 24h is fine, but must be documented + enforced deletion. also: data residency. if workspace pinned to EU region, export must stay EU.

Chloe Martin: we can place export artifacts in the same region as the workspace’s telemetry warehouse partition (EU/US). but beta accounts might not have strict residency; still should not cross region silently.

Marcus Lin: please ensure behavior is: export runs in workspace region; if region unknown -> default to US but BLOCK restricted workspaces (fintech etc) until region config is set.

Evan Scott: FYI Northstar fintech (beta candidate) explicitly asked about audit logs + residency for downloads. if we can’t answer cleanly, they’ll hold.

Paula Mendes: noted. we can move Northstar to later beta tranche if needed.

Kevin Osei: audit logging: we need at least two events:
1) export job created
2) export file downloaded
plus optional: share link created + share link accessed.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: we can emit to central audit log sink. fields must include actor + target + request metadata. what exact fields do you want?

Kevin Osei: minimum for export created:
- event_type = `usage_insights.export.created`
- actor_user_id / actor_email
- workspace_id, project_id (if filter)
- export_job_id
- time_range_start/end + timezone
- filters used (route/model/deployment mode/region) *BUT careful about payload size*
- result_format (csv)
- estimated_row_count (if known) or "unknown"

Kevin Osei: for export downloaded:
- event_type = `usage_insights.export.downloaded`
- actor_user_id / actor_email
- workspace_id
- export_job_id
- ip, user_agent
- download_status (success/denied/expired)

Marcus Lin: +1 and add `permission_check_result`? at least for denied attempts.

Chloe Martin: storing full filter set might be a lot (route lists). can we log a normalized summary?

Kevin Osei: yep, summary is fine. suggestion:
- `filter_dimensions` as comma-separated list (route, model, region, deployment_mode)
- `filter_values_truncated=true/false`
- `query_hash` (sha256 of canonical request) so we can correlate later without dumping full contents

Dylan Brooks: that’s doable.

Cole Summers: also add `billing_period` or whether it’s “usage window” vs “invoice month”? finance folks will ask when reconciling.

Paula Mendes: + include `data_freshness_ts` or `aggregation_lag_seconds` when export generated? otherwise people will claim mismatch.

Dylan Brooks: ok, we can include `data_freshness_ts` in export response + log it in created event.

Connor O'Brien: release side: if audit log fields aren’t ready, we should gate beta enablement. can someone state what’s blocking vs required for allowlist?

Marcus Lin: required for beta allowlist:
- RBAC enforcement on API for cost view + export
- signed URL w/ short TTL
- export file TTL + deletion
- audit logs for created + downloaded with fields we just listed (hash ok)
- share links must require auth + perms

SecurityBot: Reminder: Please attach security review checklist to the change request. Template: https://confluence.redwood.example/security/review-checklist (internal)

Kaitlyn Nguyen: question: workspace setting semantics. if `cost_visibility_enabled=false`, do we return 403 for cost endpoints, or return usage-only (no $ fields)?

Kevin Osei: prefer 403 for cost endpoints, but for the main breakdown endpoint maybe return usage-only if caller lacks cost perms. avoid leaking cost via partial fields.

Chloe Martin: we have two endpoints planned: `/breakdown` (usage + cost fields) and `/usage_breakdown` (usage-only). can route based on permission.

Dylan Brooks: that’s cleaner. console can call usage-only and then upgrade to cost view if permitted.

Marcus Lin: ok. last thing: “who can export cost” includes service accounts. are we allowing API tokens to export?

Chloe Martin: yes, if token has permission. but we can default to disallow for “restricted workspaces” until we add extra controls.

Kevin Osei: service accounts exporting cost is a big exfil path. minimum: enforce same RBAC + require MFA? (not possible for service accounts). suggestion for beta: only user sessions can export; block PAT/service tokens.

Dylan Brooks: blocking non-interactive tokens for export is possible (check auth principal type). might break a couple internal workflows though.

Evan Scott: customers asked for programmatic export to pipe into BI. blocking tokens might be a dealbreaker for some.

Marcus Lin: compromise for beta:
- allow programmatic export only for workspace Owners/Admins creating a “reporting” service account, but require explicit enablement flag per workspace (`allow_programmatic_exports=true`). if we can’t ship the flag, default deny programmatic export.

Paula Mendes: we can live with default deny for beta and tell customers it’s coming in GA.

Dylan Brooks: ok, will implement: export endpoint rejects service principals unless workspace allowlist flag set (can be internal-only config initially).

Kevin Osei: works.

Connor O'Brien: can someone drop action items + owners so i can add to rollout checklist?

Dylan Brooks: action items:
- Dylan/Chloe: add `usage_insights.view_cost` + `usage_insights.export` checks server-side; split usage-only endpoint
- Kaitlyn: role mapping + permission docs
- Dylan: export TTL deletion job (24h) + signed URL TTL (15m)
- Dylan: audit events `export.created` + `export.downloaded` with query_hash + freshness_ts
- Dylan: deny programmatic exports by default (service principals), add internal workspace override

Marcus Lin: please also add: share links must require auth + perms at access time. no “anyone with link”.

Chloe Martin: ack. share link will just be a saved report id in DB; no public token.

Kevin Osei: once you have a draft of audit fields, tag me for review. :thumbsup:

Dylan Brooks: will do. targeting end of week for PR so we can re-review before enabling first beta accounts.

Marcus Lin: thanks all. keeping this thread open for any follow-ups.
